aa23a462a0f20d8f.note: Johnson-Lindenstrauss (Achlioptas sparse) + turbo + polar quant of an input vector; each returns quant codes/projection (sketch) plus a sha16 fingerprint (hash).

13ade817c6e3ab52.note: bhIndexLinear: linear Brown-Hilbert render scalar = sector*(WIDTH*3) + lane*WIDTH + glyph; companion reg(name) supplies the sector/lane/glyph triple.

aa70e961e245e57d.note: Single-cylinder pigeonhole readability ceiling: given slice size N (scalar) and render width W=SECTORS*WIDTH*3 (scalar), a distance collision is forced once C(N,2)>W i.e. N >= sqrt(2W). Returns the ceiling N* (scalar) / collision-forced verdict.

Well-defined iff uniqueness holds; head ~3/7 on 100B totals.

bf02b9e64f3094bc.note: Turbo uniform scalar quant: min/max normalize the vector into integer codes at 'bits' resolution, returns the quant sketch (codes + metadata scale/min/max) and a sha16 fingerprint hash. arity 2 = (vector, bits scalar in options).

a3b556e29c6ef0ef.note: Polar pair quant: pairs consecutive components into (radius,angle), quantizes radius by maxRadius/levels and angle over 2pi into integer code pairs, returns the polar sketch plus sha16 fingerprint hash. arity 2 = (vector, bits scalar in options).

afc840609a7af694.note: quantBits clamp (plus round/finiteNumber helpers): coerces a value to a finite number and clamps the bit-depth scalar into [1,24]. Pure scalar->scalar guard used by all quant formulas.

cb9646b0748a6375.note: Triple-quant reconstruction (inverse of tripleQuant): reads the sketch's code triples + metadata scales (radius/theta/phi), maps each back through spherical->cartesian, returns the reconstructed coordinate vector trimmed to packet.dimension. Depends on round/finiteNumber.

5e96c20af2be5051.note: vectorFromText: hashes a text/name string into utf8 bytes, then derives a fixed-dimension deterministic embedding vector by indexing those bytes; returns the vector.

486fee6442cd89f6.note: ZERO_COUNTERS: a frozen constant set of E=0 invariant counters (all zero); pure declaration, no inputs, emits the counter set.

472a3550b644d9a1.note: Absorption-descriptor: over a set of HRM source artifacts, emit a git commit (hash) and a counts table (set of metric scalars, all runtime/mutation counts = 0). Descriptor-only, E=0.

59d2def680ca67a7.note: algorithm: GLYPH-GENESIS BEHCS-256 minter. glyph(key,alphabet): hashes key (name) via sha256, reads first 8 bytes big-endian u64, base-256 expands against the alphabet (set) into an 8-glyph string. Outputs a glyph.

790034779306b951.note: ratio table: CALIBRATION_ROWS constant — measured quant sketch benchmark rows (sha_gain/write_gain/compare_gain scalars across 1MB..2048MB payloads). No inputs; a fixed set of measured scalar ratios over the quant sketch.

eb755cbec1e7fad7.note: law: head encode-rate flatness — constant assertion that head-encode throughput is flat 1.8-2.1 GBps from 256MB to 2GB (head tax exactly linear, never degrades). No inputs; states a scalar-rate invariant.

7a2e59564cfbe8e8.note: triple-spherical quant: (x,y,z) triples (vector) + bits (scalar) -> per-triple [r,theta,phi] codes = quant sketch. TripleMetadata holds radius/theta/phi scales.

4d9cd743289790d4.note: sovlinux PID-1 boot gate: reads MANIFEST.hbp (bytes), computes Sha256 and compares to build-baked EXPECTED_MANIFEST_SHA256 (hash). Output is the boot/health gate decision (scalar pass-fail; PID-1 no-respawn on fail). Core formula = sha256(manifest)==expected.

9ea0b118c9001a6a.note: Recurrence corrective gate, depth-7 (PRIME) branching-2 tree: build(tamperAddr) where addr is a name. Each node mints agent+watcher pids via sha16, recomputes truth=sha16('work|'+addr), gate_ok = reported==trueVal, subtree_ok rolls up. Returns the verified node tree (set of gated nodes) plus nodeCount.

4c7e6a9cdabfac8b.note: depth-3 branching-3 (3^3=27 leaf) self-reflect tree: runTree(tamperPath) where tamperPath is a node-path name. Leaves do truth(addr)=sha16 mod 1e6, watcher recomputes, gate_ok=reported==watcherTruth; supervisors authorize iff every child passed, roll up sha16. Returns the gated node tree (set) with failing-path list.

a73b9c288f2ca2f2.note: bhIndexSidon prime-cube embedding (Golomb/Sidon idea): from a reg record r={sector,lane,glyph}, returns sector*p0^3 + lane*p3^3 + glyph*p6^3 using DISTINCT prime cubes (PRIMES[13,17,23,31,41,47,73,79,83,89,131]) so channels are linearly independent over the integers = a Sidon Brown-Hilbert linear index. Depends on prime cubes.

2c864ec8f4d8577a.note: domain_for_cp / supervisor_for_cp / lane_for_cp: range-partition a glyph codepoint (0-1023) into 8 atlas bands -> domain+supervisor sector band and a lane. glyph -> sector,lane.

76e3994c6adf8238.note: parse_pid(pid): split the BEHCS-1024 PID grammar (role-name-PID-Hhex-A##-W###-P##-N#####) into structured parts; extracts role/name, activity/wave (sector-like), lane region, and prime block. pid -> name,sector,lane,prime.

547a92651f9a426e.note: pidToSliceCoord(pid): sha256(pid) hashed into a 50D coord = 16x3 barycentric simplex weights + layer(0..25) + mtp_offset(0..2). hash->manifold address of a frozen Gemma slice.

488b8777bdb32b60.note: mineReverseGain(env): reads env.glyph + the jl vector; score = sign(+1, or -1 if glyph in MASK_GLYPHS) * ||jl vector||. Signed-distance reverse-gain scalar.

b280fe467f7b1510.note: supervisor-collision-router: takes a set of colliding entities (each carrying a name/id via entityId and a bh_index via bandOf), classifies into 5 states (real-collision vs logical-preserved vs namespace-split), routes to a decision name, and emits an HBP pipe-row whose row_hash is sha16 over the prefix.

6f8cff412a82471e.note: dispatchHbpRow(env, kind): builds a DISPATCH{PENDING,FIRED,VETO} HBP wire-row from the dispatch envelope's pid, bh_addr (behcs1024 brown_hilbert_address), score scalar, and kind/action name; appends json=0 then row_hash=sha16(prefix). Same hbpRow grammar as the collision-router.

614aac3dd5ff6b2a.note: spawnWorker(i): rotor index i selects prime=PRIMES[i%7], then model name=MODELS[prime%2], spawns the worker and returns the FREE_AGENT_OUTPUT envelope (set/payload). Scalar slot index in -> prime + model name + envelope set out.

3579cd3152d756ed.note: GNN glyph-op family (RESET-HALVE toward 0.5, CONVERGE-GATE, REVERSE-GAIN, MASK-FLIP=-bias): takes a glyph operand plus its current scalar weight/bias and returns the updated scalar.

ce1d064bfe74c6d9.note: D11 proof-level lattice: maps a claim identifier (name) to its evidence level (PROVEN/OBSERVED/INHERITED/ASSUMED) -- a name out. Pure taxonomy lookup over the levels set.

52cd7c674fd4d30c.note: vonMangoldtNext: divisor set divs + cp scalar + seed scalar + step scalar -> normalized Lambda(q)/log(cp) weights vector, then deterministic-unit pick returning next/divisor/weight (scalars) plus a reason name. Depends on vonMangoldt (4e7bdd1e).

40d9a70354b67195.note: Hilbert-collision canon: qualifies a bh_index by vantage name (ACER/LIRIS/SHARED) against DISPUTED_BANDS {lo:930,hi:1229}. Body exports the vantage set + disputed band set used to gate a bh_index binding; returns the disputed-band set membership. address-collision-canon kind.

6f0f0c6ce89d8c89.note: SCOPES table maps a scope name (read/attest/bind-draft/mint/write) to its escalates scalar (0 or 1). Lookup by name -> escalation scalar. auth-scope/safety-gate kind.

8707d1a45892d12a.note: polarQuant(vector, options.bits scalar): pairs values into radius/angle, quantizes to code pairs (the polar quant sketch) with metadata, plus sha16 fingerprint hash. quant-polar-pair formula.

a40724a659b8a7c2.note: tripleQuant(vector, options.bits scalar): triples values into radius/theta/phi, quantizes to code triples (the quant sketch) with coarse/medium/fine lanes and metadata, plus sha16 fingerprint hash. quant-triple-spherical formula.

489ff4ce6cfca47f.note: hilbertIndex (xy2d): maps two grid coordinates (x,y) + bits scalar to a 1D Brown-Hilbert linear index.

5e9b259d8007c263.note: BEHCS-256 PID tuple (actor,device,lane,prime): actor name + device sha256-12 hash + lane + prime, Hilbert-mapped to one 1D PID index; collide iff tuples equal.

b5d1f9a11233ef1a.note: quant HEAD-tax/TAIL-gain benchmark ladder: runs the 3.1KB quant sketch over message bytes (1MB..2GB) and emits a set of QUANTBENCHRESULT receipt rows (head_ms, sha_gain, write/compare gains).

6a520f8c0920e9a7.note: readHbpHead: reads file bytes, takes head-length scalar (default 4096), returns sha256_head (hash) over the leading slice plus size (scalar) and path (name). Body returns {path,size,sha256_head,preview_utf8}.

92e76412f10a1bee.note: os-on-metal 7-layer fusion CFG: resolves env-var keys (name) to a set of named endpoint URLs/paths (fabric_url, hermes_store, anthropic/gemini urls, kernel roots, hookwall, cosign path). Body is a constant object literal => set of named layers.

9383bad28dfdf68e.note: Bijective PID mint: (actor name, device name, lane, prime) -> pid as a Hilbert-curve tuple, zero collisions by construction; the lazy-materialized 100B PIDs index into a Brown-Hilbert linear bh_index. 61MB prime-list seed = Big-Pickle.

fb4dccee2cdf692b.note: 10-byte SHA cube addressing / address-compression: hash agent-state bytes to a 10-byte cryptographic fingerprint (hash), then place it in Brown-Hilbert space as coord + linear bh_index. Generates ~10^200 structural paths via geometry, not live compute; 2TB USB maps billions of compact keys.

8d77d3fc23ae110a.note: system-throughput-summary: prose descriptor of the OS; nullary producer emitting fixed scalar metrics (100M agents, 39k/sec, 230M mistakes). No deterministic inputs; not a callable formula.

d16c495b7d261c6e.note: pidMap: slices a payloadHash hex string into x,y (coord), room%10000 (sector), pid:slice(0,16); calls hilbertIndex(x,y) for the bh_index. Depends on the Hilbert index formula.

d99a2c38bc5ae3fa.note: BEHCS-1024 supervisor band->quant atlas table: a cp linear position index (Brown-Hilbert/coordinate-position band) maps to a quant-scheme name + domain name + supervisor name (e.g. 256-383 -> triple/sovereignty/gaia). Lookup: bh_index -> name. arity=1. Static atlas, no callee deps.

6d36a46f508f8d8d.note: Eight quant engines roster spec: Polar/Turbo/JL/Zeta/Triple/Quadruple/JS/von-Mangoldt. Body is a named collection of engines; given a roster name it enumerates the set of engine identifiers.

d99bf9a918c039a7.note: Static census-receipt literal: BODY is a JSON totals block returning grand_count=15020 and grand_bytes=5390074631 (two scalars) plus biggest/oldest file metadata. No function args, so arity 0; outputs are the two scalar totals the body holds.

5064c273ccc610b8.note: HBP heartbeat vs per-agent token economy: BODY consumes projN (one scalar) with const BATCH=1e6 to compute heartbeatRows and the antipattern ratio. Outputs are scalar row-count and scalar ratio (the 'xfewer' factor) it pushes to the log.

5ecc0fe53d049aea.note: Fabric/helm endpoint topology: BODY is a static array literal of named endpoint URLs (acer-primary, liris-mirror, pool, omnispindle, helm-4920->4922->4924). No args; yields a set of endpoints each carrying a name identifier.

9919caaf4b6b371f.note: reciprocal_rank_fusion(search_results_dict, k=60): inputs are the per-query results collection (set) and the constant k (scalar). Returns reranked_results = a set ordered by fused scalar scores. Arity 2; outputs set + scalar (fused score).

7c0cf9595ff8f2ab.note: Shannon engine beat counts: kind=constant. Literal object {v4:2592, v5:93312} beats; nullary, emits fixed scalar counts. No composition inputs.

dd7d15022d8c78a7.note: citizenIdentity: from a citizen identifier derives MODEL-* pid (sha16 hash), glyph (sha16), cube_cell (sector cell), and bh_3d_idx via hilbertEncode (Hilbert nD encoder 149).

6ca808d777d511cb.note: rotate: round-robin prism cursor over a citizen set (opts/census), picks next ready citizen for an intent name, spreads citizenIdentity (pid/glyph/cube_cell/bh) plus rotation_idx scalar.

6c0699f0d2611127.note: BEHCS glyph alphabet: makeGlyphs(n) builds an n-length frozen set of cpXXXX glyph names; BEHCS256/1024 subset embeddings. scalar size -> set of glyphs.

7b5be3ba751f16c3.note: Hilbert nD encode (Skilling 2004): transposeToAxesND / axesToTransposeND mutate transpose-array X for n dims, b bits, converting between linear bh_index transpose form and per-axis coord. Encoder underlying citizenIdentity bh.

c4dd809126bb9a0c.note: sieveFirstNPrimes(n): scalar count n in, Eratosthenes sieve bounded by Rosser p_n<n(ln n+ln ln n); BODY returns out[] = first-n primes, a set of primes.

4830d780c6e979f5.note: mtpHeads(cp0): cp0 codepoint/glyph-index in (opts optional); spawns K zeta heads, each predicts trajectory→final cp scalar + Brown-Hilbert bh_coord. BODY returns heads set.

993e660319998a21.note: HRM slow-loop shape classifier: maps (envelopeType name, agentProf name, currentCp scalar, history set) to a shape name + cp-band via deterministic string-pattern/history-axis matching; returns shapeRecord(shape, currentCp).

351d10ff7695c1a1.note: helm-worker http-proxy backend: takes worker config (set, holds httpUrl) and job prompt (name), POSTs prompt, returns response body (bytes) plus exitCode/durationMs (scalar).

76334743fa0ff5e5.note: chunk PID composition: builds `BH.NEW100B.PID.${c}.L${lane}.${topGpid.toString(16)}` from counter c (scalar), lane, and topGpid (scalar) into the Brown-Hilbert chunk pid.

969057f63568d79f.note: sectorDescriptor: from sector derives cylinder = nthPrime(sector+1) (prime), engine lane=sector%3, glyph=sector%WIDTH, and a routed mode/class name; reuses classifyBhIndex and laneFor.

c70b48d515b30414.note: STE cross-tower band anchor: for each tower prime p, W=p^3, Sidon set erdosTuran(p) scaled by W, then a GUARD=2^40 super-increasing accumulator (scalar) lays anchors so addresses never cross-collide. Inputs: tower primes, Sidon set, GUARD scalar; emits the linear band addresses (bh_index) and the full collision-free address set.

51d9ac1a2d2dc52d.note: Stacked identity scheme: AddressID=PID=sha256(canonical(A))[0:16] (pid from bytes), SliceDistanceID=H(ProjectionCert,d) over a distance scalar and the cert hash, RelationKey/RelationEvent/ProjectionCert = H(...) geometry-free names. Given canonical bytes, a distance scalar, and the cert hash, returns the pid and the derived relation hashes.

c66fa744f7d4e55c.note: timedLoop: runs scalar iteration-count times, each step consuming a per-iteration byte payload (byteLength accumulated mod 1e9+7) to fold a checksum and timing scalars; returns scalar checksum/timings.

3ac367f1ba4af81d.note: benchmark: from a vector dimension plus raw and fast packet byte sizes, computes scalar size/speedup ratios and a sha16 fingerprint hash over the result packet.

18422fdc9ef279a1.note: zero-loss boundary invariant: asserts receipt_zero_loss holds iff the payload_hash is preserved (numeric quantization approximate); reduces a hash-equality check to a scalar boolean.

1d6a2162e2e893be.note: target truth boundary invariant: composes the ZERO_COUNTERS set with boolean truth-boundary flags (cpu active, gpu inactive) into an emitted boundary set.

d960dea7a0de5235.note: BEHCS native-space room: maps a room name/id to its 6-axis coordinate tuple, a BEHCS glyph, and its BH.SPACE pid.

6ba8a94785364492.note: HRM input embedding scaling: from hidden_size scalar computes embed_scale=sqrt(hidden_size) and init_std=1/embed_scale, then initializes scaled token/puzzle/position embedding vectors.

936afba07ed7be77.note: ACT halting: new_steps scalar vs halt_max_steps, plus q_halt/q_continue logit vectors; body returns the halted boolean mask (set of halted slots).

741dd8c9fe1601bb.note: Bootstrap target-Q: sigmoid of max(next_q_halt_logits, next_q_continue_logits) gated by is_last_step; two logit vectors in, target_q_continue vector out. Depends on halting step state.

12ba70076853e4eb.note: q_halt accuracy metric: counts where (q_halt_logits>=0)==seq_is_correct under valid mask; logit vector + correctness set in, scalar count out.

e768dd198de7b3bd.note: PID-distance cylinder-stack: pidToCylinderStack(pid,k) hashes pid per prime-index i in 1..k into {radius,angle,height} -> vector of k cylinders; distanceBetweenPIDs returns a scalar distance.

8fe67f45e10d2555.note: Brown-Hilbert dimensionality spec (live=47D, proposal=50D): folds a high-D embedding vector into a single Brown-Hilbert linear index per hyperglyph-map.public.v1.json.

f6f5c53d6a9702ab.note: Hookwall gate composition: pipes raw message bytes through GC+super-gulp+reverse-gain-GNN+omnishannon+omnihermes+omniGNN+crop-paper, yielding a gated GNN feature vector (behcs-bus-4947).

4c1bacd842bcbd6d.note: 100B lazy-mint: mintPID(index)=sha16(Hilbert -> actor.device.lane.prime). Takes a linear Hilbert index plus lane and prime, hashes to a 16-hex pid. Cold tier = formula-only/regenerable.

9671981931e3669d.note: provenance(): builds the self-describing tuple (set of who/what/where/when/why/how + machine) and mints its pid via mintPID(actor,{device,lane,prime}). who/device collapse to name; returns the full record (set) carrying a pid field.

037e5cd82d7b4436.note: prime-sector allocator address format BH.SECTOR.P{prime}.R{room}.{sha16}: composes a sector, a prime, and a 16-hex id (the sha16/pid slot) into the canonical Brown-Hilbert address string (name).

5bbaf0aba8b8a606.note: Page-store interface: put(pid,value)/get(pid)/scanByPID(prefix)/compact(never-delete). Keyed by pid with byte values; scan/compact yield collections (set). Primary signature is pid-keyed put.

32ee872ec557e063.note: state() constant descriptor: template_count and per-template/spindle/row scalars plus chain_id_format name string; no inputs.

6271ca73f0553d18.note: Constant HBPv1 authority row: carries a PID-tuple and a terminal row_hash with all execute/export flags=0 (describe_only); emits pid + row_hash.

ff5e1f3d4752d8a1.note: algorithm: vonMangoldtNext(cp,opts). cp=scalar, opts=set (seed/step). Computes divisors of cp, weights each by von Mangoldt Lambda(q)/log(cp), deterministic weighted descent picks a divisor and returns {next scalar, divisor (prime-power divisor), weight scalar}.

3568a57ed6858ea6.note: whiteroomCubeAddress: one envelope (set) -> sha256 hex hash -> layer=sha%3, axis1=sha%6, axis2=sha%6 forming cube_address [layer,axis1,axis2] = a 3D coord. Body returns {sha, cube_address}; primary product is the coord.

1f43f08001842c80.note: quant-bus G1/G2/G3/G4 composition: one env (set) -> G2 mineForward path_score scalar (+ gnn_forward_quant sha16 hash), G3 mineReverseGain reverse_gain scalar (717f...) -> gnn_reverse_quant hash, G4 transition (f960...) next glsm state name. Aggregates scalar metrics + state name + sha16 quant hashes onto env.

d8b54b23b4adaaa4.note: gnnForward(env): caller_pid(pid)+target/verb(name)+cube_47d(vector) -> node_embedding via sha16(=hash) and edge_weight = sha16(antecedents) top-4-hex/65535 (=scalar in [0,1]). reverseGainGnn extends edge_weight by priority into gain_magnitude(scalar). Body returns {node_embedding:hash, edge_weight:scalar}.

67aee0eb5656cc2d.note: Brown-Hilbert prime-log embedding + tower rotation: maps slot index n (scalar) under tower profile rho_tau (name) over the prime axes (prime, contributing log p_i/p_i) to the d-dim embedding x_i(n,tau) (vector), which projects to a Brown-Hilbert point (coord). Cross-tower squared distance D-squared is a derived scalar from the vectors.

d3993712235f1519.note: D11 proof-level stamp catalog: the body is the levels object (set of PROVEN/OBSERVED/INHERITED/ASSUMED keyed by name). Stamps a claim identifier (name) against the catalog, returning the assigned proof-level (name) drawn from the level set; the catalog itself is the set.

accepted.name: glyph-genesis 8-glyph host derivation (BEHCS-256)
accepted.note: glyph(key,alphabet): sha256 the name string, read first 8 bytes big-endian as u64, emit 8 glyph chars by repeated base-256 modulo over the alphabet set. name+set -> 8-glyph string.

accepted.name: Tool-citizen sha256 -> BEHCS-256/1024 slot
accepted.note: sha256(TOOL name) hex; slice[0:8]%256 = BEHCS-256 glyph slot, slice[8:16]%1024 = BEHCS-1024 glyph slot. name -> two modular glyph indices.

accepted.name: BEHCS-1024 subset-embedding (1024->256 fold)
accepted.note: subset_embedding_cp(cp): fold a 1024-space glyph codepoint into the 256-space via cp%256, returns None if cp>=1024. glyph(1024) -> glyph(256).

accepted.name: mintPid core PID seed math
accepted.note: mintPid core: sha256(name) seeds the triad; derives hex head, seed u32, then lane=seed%3, sector=seed%113, glyph_1024=seed%WIDTH, hilbert index, and cube_bh BH.sector.lane.glyph. tier+nest are the scalar args, prime is the P block. name+prime+scalar -> full pid plus glyph/sector/lane/bh_index.

accepted.name: mintPID — deterministic PID minter
accepted.note: mintPID mintFromTuple({actor,device,lane,prime,alphabet}): validates actor glyph-slot in [0,alphabet), device name string, lane in the 7-lane set, prime>=2, alphabet scalar (256|1024); subset-embeds and sha16-mints the deterministic PID. glyph+name+lane+prime+scalar -> pid.

accepted.name: PID fingerprint sha16
accepted.note: pid_fingerprint_sha16(pid): sha256 the pid bytes, hex-encode first 8 bytes -> 16-hex fingerprint hash. pid -> hash.

accepted.name: Phase-1 ed25519-placeholder sign/derive (sha-derived)
accepted.note: expected_sig_bytes(public,msg): Phase-1 sha-derived signature placeholder; sha256(public||':sign:'||msg)=half_a, sha256(half_a)=half_b, concat to 64-byte signature. pubkey bytes + message bytes -> signature bytes (ed25519-dalek deferred).

accepted.name: cosign chain link (prev_sha hash-chain)
accepted.note: cosign chain link: an envelope record binding a set of artifacts (each path+sha256) to the prior link via prev_sha; the link's own sha forms the next prev_sha. prev hash + artifact set -> chained hash.

accepted.name: Brown-Hilbert curve xy2d / d2xy (10k-room indexing)
accepted.note: xy2d(n,x,y): grid size n + 2D coords (x,y) folded bit-by-bit into the Brown-Hilbert linear index d via (3*rx)^ry accumulation. Pure; inverse d2xy/roomToHilbert in same module go bh_index->coord.

accepted.name: GLSM latent state machine transition rule
accepted.note: transition(prev_state, {edge_weight, path_confidence, reverse_gain}): pure latent-FSM step. prev_state is a state name; three finite scalars gate advancement DESCRIBED->EDGE_MINED->PATH_FOUND->...->CONVERGED(absorbing). Returns next state name. reverse_gain comes from the GNN scorer.

accepted.name: omniquant triple/polar/turbo/JL adapter
accepted.note: quantMetadata(env, mode): vectorFromEnvelope yields the metadata vector; mode name selects triple/polar/turbo/jl quantizer; returns the quant packet (sketch). packQuantPacket would then go sketch->bytes.

accepted.name: omniQuantScore pure-integer quantizer
accepted.note: omniQuantScore = parseInt(sha(key)[0:4],16)%1001: hashes a key name into a pure-integer score in [0,1000], no float/IEEE drift. The QUANT-delta gate scalar.

accepted.name: exFAT EntrySetChecksum (rotate-right-add u16)
accepted.note: entry_set_checksum(entries: &[u8]): rotate-right-by-1 then wrapping-add each byte (skipping primary-entry checksum field idx 2,3) into a u16 rolling checksum. bytes->hash.

accepted.name: exFAT up-cased NameHash (rotate-right-add u16 over LE bytes)
accepted.note: name_hash(upcased_utf16): up-cased UTF-16 name unit bytes (lo,hi) rotate-right-add into a u16 rolling hash, matching exFAT on-disk name-hash. name->hash.

accepted.name: device-agent BH coords (16 levels x trio)
accepted.note: DEVREG row builder (Brown-Hilbert pre-existence-graph exporter): device name + level scalar + role name -> agent pid, bh_index, glyph index, and device_handle hash. Carries hilbert/phase too but the load-bearing address is pid+bh_index+glyph+handle.

accepted.name: classifyBhIndex prime-power classifier (von-Mangoldt support)
accepted.note: classifyBhIndex(index): returns lane=index%3 and a prime-power class name (unit/prime/composite/p2/p3/pk) via von-Mangoldt-style smallest-factor trial division. bh_index in -> lane + class-name out.

accepted.name: isPrime 6k+-1 wheel
accepted.note: isPrime(n) 6k+-1 wheel: scalar n in -> boolean (scalar) primality out. Body returns true/false, not a prime value, so output is scalar not prime.

accepted.name: vonMangoldt
accepted.note: vonMangoldt(n) = log p if n=p^a else 0. Scalar n in -> scalar Lambda(n) out. Uses PRIME_SET/PRIMES internally for the prime-power check.

accepted.name: nuLambda invariant measure
accepted.note: nuLambda(cp) invariant measure = 1/(x*log x) for x=Number(cp)>=2, else 0. Scalar in -> scalar density out.

accepted.name: von_mangoldt_lambda
accepted.note: von_mangoldt_lambda harness: inlines its own isPrime + vonMangoldt (no external dep). Scalar n in -> scalar Lambda(n)=log p if n=p^a else 0 out.

accepted.name: eight quant engines
accepted.note: Declares the canonical set of 8 quant-engine names (Polar/Turbo/JL/Zeta/Triple/Quadruple/JS/von-Mangoldt). No params; body is a name-listing, so it emits a set of engine names. quant-engine-set kind.

accepted.name: agent-spawn throughput tiers + 100B run
accepted.note: Frozen CALIBRATION_ROWS: spawn tiers A-F with scalar throughput rates plus receipt rows carrying sha16 hashes. Constants only (default no-live-benchmark), so arity 0; returns the set of benchmark rows (scalar rates + hash sha16). scale-benchmark kind.

accepted.name: sentence()/grammar glyph tuple builder
accepted.note: sentence(fields): takes a set of dim->value field entries and maps each to {dim, tuple, glyph} via glyph(d+':'+v). Input is the fields set; returns a set of dimensional glyph tuples. behcs-256 glyph grammar builder.

accepted.name: Johnson-Lindenstrauss Achlioptas sparse projection
accepted.note: johnsonLindenstrauss(vector, options): Achlioptas sparse projection using seed name + targetDimension scalar; achlioptasWeight derives weights from a sha256 digest. Returns the projection vector plus a sha16 fingerprint hash. quant-johnson-lindenstrauss kind.

accepted.name: Turbo uniform quant
accepted.note: turboQuant(vector, options.bits scalar): uniform min/max scalar quantization into integer codes (the quant sketch) with metadata, plus sha16 fingerprint hash. quant-turbo-uniform formula.

accepted.name: Triple-spherical reconstruction (inverse)
accepted.note: reconstructTriple(packet): inverse of tripleQuant; takes the quant sketch packet (codes + metadata radius/theta/phi scales) and reconstructs the x/y/z vector. Depends on the triple-spherical quant packet shape. quant-triple-reconstruct formula.

accepted.name: Vector normalization (L2)
accepted.note: normalizeVector: L2-normalize a vector; returns same-dimension unit vector (zero vector if norm 0).

accepted.name: vectorFromText deterministic embedding from bytes
accepted.note: vectorFromText: deterministic embedding from UTF-8 bytes of a name/text string plus dimension scalar; returns vector.

accepted.name: pidMap from payload hash (PID + room + Hilbert coords)
accepted.note: pidMap: slices a payload hash into pid, room name, Hilbert x/y coord and bh_index (calls hilbertIndex).

accepted.name: HBS1 central substrate frame (length-prefixed append-only)
accepted.note: HBS1 appendSubstratePacket: length-prefixed append-only store frame from an envelope/receipt packet; returns written bytes, receipt fingerprint hash, store-size scalar.

accepted.name: GNN edge schema + fingerprint
accepted.note: gnnEdges: builds GNN edge rows from a pid, room name and set of lanes; each edge carries a sha16 fingerprint, grants_authority=false.

accepted.name: behcs-256 receipt schema header
accepted.note: behcs-256 receipt schema header (encoding): pid + cohort/shard name + cosign_seq_floor scalar serialized into a receipt record (bytes).

accepted.name: buildFastPacket zero-loss boundary
accepted.note: buildFastPacket: input vector (or vectorFromText fallback) + options set -> triple-quant sketch + payload_hash and packet fingerprint hash; numeric zero-loss false.

accepted.name: PID address-coordinate minter (core)
accepted.note: mintPid (core): single params object {role,name,tier,nest,kind,prime} -> pid plus glyph_1024, sector(113), lane(mod3), cube_bh/hilbert bh_index, sha16 hash.

accepted.name: agent-type separation law
accepted.note: classifyAgentType({yin_yang,prime}): yin_yang label (name) + prime parsed to int; returns agent-type string LOGICAL-WAVE / REAL-FREE / FROZEN-BRAIN (name). Branch on yin_yang==='logical' then prime parity.

accepted.name: Sidon-Tower Embedding (STE) distance-uniqueness
accepted.note: Sidon-Tower Embedding: per tower prime p, S_p={2pk+(k^2 mod p)} weighted by prime-cube p^3 plus super-increasing anchor; Phi(point)=anchor+sidon_value*p^3 -> a collision-free linear bh_index. Inputs prime p and scalar point/k.

accepted.name: three named laws: memory / slice / tail-O(1)
accepted.note: memory/slice/tail-O(1) laws: addr(e)=(H(PID(e)),t(e)) maps an event pid plus time scalar to a bijective hash address giving O(1) read; storage O(distinct artifacts) not O(positions).

accepted.name: falcon 8byte-host emission triad (md5 derivation)
accepted.note: falcon 8byte-host emission triad: from payload bytes, REAL=md5(payload), REFL=md5(REAL:self-reflect), FABR=md5(file:ask-fabric), PID0=md5(REAL||REFL||FABR) collapsing the rule-of-three triad into one supervisor pid; one append-only EMIT row.

accepted.name: 8byte-host rule-of-three triad
accepted.note: 8byte-host rule-of-three triad formula: REAL=md5(payload)[0:16], REFL=md5(REAL:self-reflect)[0:16], FABR=md5(file:ask-fabric)[0:16], PID0=md5(REAL||REFL||FABR)[0:16]; worker/reflector/fabric triad collapses to one supervisor pid commuting over all three.

accepted.name: omni address-space cardinality (capacity proof)
accepted.note: omni address-space cardinality proof: points = G^D (generator G in {256,1024,2048}, dim D=60) as exact BigInt scalar; e.g. 1024^60 ~ 10^180, exceeding observable-universe atoms. Pure capacity scalar from two scalars.

accepted.name: omni conversational-language-space cardinality
accepted.note: omni conversational-language-space cardinality: word vocab V=G^D, sentence/conversation space = V^k; given generator G, dim D, word-count k returns exact BigInt scalar (e.g. V^50 ~ 10^9030).

accepted.name: five-primitive canonical kernel reduction
accepted.note: five-primitive canonical kernel reduction: composes the set of primitive modules ADDRESS/CONTENT/INTEGRITY/SCORE/ROUTE (+hookwall entry gate, full loop) into one frozen KERNEL set exposing the five primitives.

accepted.name: Triple-quant spherical reconstruction (inverse)
accepted.note: reconstructTripleQuant(record): inverse triple-quant. Takes record.codes (per-element [radiusCode,thetaCode,phiCode] quant sketch) + metadata scales, converts each spherical code triple back to Cartesian x/y/z and flatMaps, sliced to dimension. Returns a flat array of reconstructed 3D coords = a coord vector.

accepted.name: Exact triple-Z control vector (lossless reconstruction proof)
accepted.note: exactTripleZVector(length=768): builds a control/test vector of given length where every 3rd slot (index%3===2) carries floor(index/3)%256 and others are 0. Returns an array = vector (lossless-reconstruction proof test vector).

accepted.name: Deterministic text->vector embedding
accepted.note: vectorFromText(text,dimension): deterministic text->vector embedding. Reads UTF-8 bytes of the text name, indexes them with strided coprime offsets (17/31/47) modulo length, blends scaled byte values per slot. Returns an array of length=dimension = embedding vector.

accepted.name: hashBucket helper (generic SHA modular bucketing)
accepted.note: hashBucket(seed,row,col,buckets=6): generic SHA modular bucketing. sha256Hex of `seed|row|col`, takes first 12 hex chars as int mod buckets. Internally uses a hash but the returned value is a single bucket index = scalar.

accepted.name: 10000-room rotor materialization
accepted.note: 10000-room rotor materialization report (constant, not a function): markdown key/value table of materialization counts (room_count 10000, shard_count 100, materialized_room_files 40000, plane_count 17, etc.) plus all-zero gate counters. Body is a fixed set of scalar constants describing the materialized rooms.

accepted.name: BEHCS1024 Prism dimension catalog + reflection modes
accepted.note: BEHCS1024 Prism dimension catalog (constant): frozen PRISM_REFLECTION_MODES list, COMMON keyword list (behcs1024, prism, glyph, pid, D16_PID..D47_BOUNDARY dimension names), and PROFILES array of supervisor profiles. Body returns a fixed collection/catalog = set.

accepted.name: Rank-3 multi-context geometric prediction seal (HBP row contract)
accepted.note: Rank-3 multi-context geometric prediction seal (HBP row contract, constant): fixed EXPECTED_HBP_ROWS=18, EXPECTED_UPSTREAMS=6, DECISION/SELECTED_NEXT_STEP strings, and DEFAULT_INPUTS = frozen set of upstream .hbp paths each paired with a .sha256 digest. Body declares a seal over a set of hash-addressed inputs.

accepted.name: primes.mjs referenced in omnicoder integrity manifest
accepted.note: primes.mjs integrity-manifest entry (constant): single manifest line mapping the prime table file `primes.mjs` to its sha256 digest 1284ce7a...6291. Body binds a prime table to a hash; emits prime (table reference) + hash.

accepted.name: public-runtime-listen-ports
accepted.note: public-runtime-listen-ports (constant): two log lines declaring the public runtime listens on http localhost:4791 and https localhost:5444. Body is fixed port-number scalars (the listen ports).

accepted.name: bridge-behcs-256-share-target
accepted.note: bridge-behcs-256-share-target (reference, PowerShell): JOIN-LIRIS ensures named SMB shares (LirisBridge -> rayss\Asolaria-Bridge, LirisAsolaria -> rayss\Asolaria-BEHCS-256), maps net use Z: to \\DESKTOP-J99VCNH\AcerBridge, writes a join stamp. Body produces share-target identifiers/paths = name.

accepted.name: PBKDF2HMAC-SHA256 field-encryption KDF
accepted.note: PBKDF2HMAC-SHA256 KDF: derives key material from password bytes + salt bytes. Body shows PBKDF2HMAC(algorithm=SHA256), output is raw derived-key bytes.

accepted.name: SHA256 patient pseudonym (12-hex truncation)
accepted.note: SHA256 patient pseudonym: concats salt+identifier strings, sha256, truncates to 12 hex, prefixes 'PAT-' => returns pseudonym name string. Salt may originate from the KDF lane.

accepted.name: GNN edge-classifier scoring (ReLU + dropout 0.5 + concat edge features)
accepted.note: GNN edge-classifier: takes node-embedding vector x and edge_index set of (u,v) pairs, concats x[row]||x[col] per edge, edge_classifier => per-edge score vector (squeezed). Scores feed softmax.

accepted.name: Classifier probability via softmax over logits
accepted.note: softmax over logits along last dim: maps a logits vector to a probability vector. Consumes scores/logits produced upstream by the edge classifier.

accepted.name: DefaultHasher MCP tool-name hash
accepted.note: DefaultHasher MCP tool-name hash: hashes a name string via Rust DefaultHasher, formats finish() as hex => hash digest string.

accepted.name: Hilbert d2xy index (quadrant rotation)
accepted.note: Hilbert d2xy: signature (x,y,bits=16); two required scalar coordinate components (bits has a default, not a required input) => returns linear Hilbert/Brown-Hilbert index d.

accepted.name: cubeTuple 48-dim
accepted.note: cubeTuple 48-dim: reads payloadHash hex into bytes, builds 48 {dim,lane,value} entries (lanes has a default, not a required input) => a 48-element embedding vector with per-dim lane assignment.

accepted.name: Achlioptas sparse JL weight
accepted.note: Achlioptas sparse JL weight: sha256(seed|row|col), bucket = first-12-hex %6 => returns sqrt(3), -sqrt(3), or 0. Inputs: seed name + row scalar + col scalar; output scalar weight.

accepted.name: Johnson-Lindenstrauss projection
accepted.note: johnsonLindenstrauss(vector): normalizeVector(vector) then Achlioptas-weighted projection to sqrt-scaled target dim. Returns packet whose `projection` is a reduced vector plus sha16 `fingerprint` (hash). Single domain input (vector); options is a config bag, not a vocab value, so arity=1. deps: normalizeVector + sha16.

accepted.name: Polar pair quant
accepted.note: polarQuant(vector): pairs successive components into radius/angle and quantizes to [radiusCode,angleCode] `codes` (a polar quant sketch) plus sha16 `fingerprint` (hash). arity=1 (vector; options config). deps: quantBits + sha16.

accepted.name: Triple spherical quant
accepted.note: tripleQuant(vector): triples of components -> spherical radius/theta/phi codes (coarse/medium/fine lanes) returned as `codes`/`codes_flat` (a quant sketch) plus sha16 `fingerprint` (hash). arity=1 (vector; options config). deps: quantBits + sha16.

accepted.name: Triple reconstruct (inverse)
accepted.note: reconstructTriple(packet): inverse of tripleQuant. Consumes the triple-quant packet (a quant sketch with codes+metadata scales) and rebuilds Cartesian x/y/z components, returning a reconstructed vector sliced to packet.dimension. arity=1 (the sketch packet). dep: tripleQuant (the forward sketch producer).

accepted.name: normalizeVector
accepted.note: normalizeVector(vector): L2-normalize a vector (divide by its Euclidean norm; all-zero stays zero), returning a unit vector. Pure vector->vector. arity=1. Calls toVector/round which are not in the sibling-pid index, so deps=[].

accepted.name: vector L2 normalization
accepted.note: vector L2 normalization (D: drive copy of normalizeVector, byte-identical body): divide vector by its Euclidean norm to a unit vector. Pure vector->vector. arity=1. toVector/round not in sibling-pid index, deps=[].

accepted.name: HBH1 binary envelope 5-section
accepted.note: HBH1 envelope spec: assembles a set of 5 prior section outputs (1=sha256_payload, 2=JL_float32, 3=turbo_codes, 4=polar_codes, 5=triple_codes) into one binary envelope (bytes) that is sha16-addressable (hash). arity=1 (the set of sections). deps: sha16 + the four quant producers (JL/turbo/polar/triple).

accepted.name: OHQE 10K-room throughput
accepted.note: OHQE 10K-room throughput projection: rooms x 100 answers/min => 1M/10min (CPU), 10M/10min (GPU projected). A scalar room-count maps to a scalar throughput. Rooms are Brown-Hilbert-addressed but the formula itself is scalar->scalar. arity=1, no callee deps.

accepted.name: ZETA von-Mangoldt next-position chain (F1)
accepted.note: F1 von-Mangoldt chain: given pid P at hilbert coord H, treat cp (1..1024 scalar) as n_0, sample next n via divisors weighted by Lambda/log, hilbertDecode each to a bh_coord. Body returns K future hilbert_coords -> a set of coords (pre-warm next K chambers).

accepted.name: ZETA invariant-measure voxel mass (F2)
accepted.note: F2 invariant measure nu_Lambda(cp) ~= 1/(cp*log cp) computed over cp=1..1023. Input is the codepoint scalar (cp); body returns a probability distribution over all 1024 codepoints = a vector (per-band sums give supervisor CONVERGE weights).

accepted.name: ZETA quant invariant-measure (architecture row)
accepted.note: ZETA architecture row: von-Mangoldt-chain divisor-weighted next-position sampling + nu_Lambda invariant measure. Body states output = predictive position on the Brown-Hilbert lattice (predict next-K for pre-warm). Composes F1 (e899...) chain and F2 (da80...) measure.

accepted.name: von-Mangoldt chain error term at BEHCS-1024
accepted.note: Error-term row: chain error is O(1/log x); at x~=1024, 1/log(1024)~=0.14 (~14%). Input x is a scalar, body returns the error magnitude as a scalar. Characterizes the F1 (e899...) chain.

accepted.name: Quadruple-Quant stack
accepted.note: Quadruple-Quant spec (Polar+Turbo+JL+ZETA), supersedes triple-quant. A compression pipeline: takes an embedding vector and produces a packed/routed quant sketch; ZETA stage (ba65...) adds predictive position.

accepted.name: Helical per-axis projector Pi
accepted.note: Helical per-axis projector: phi_i(n)=2pi*(n mod p_i)/p_i, rho_i(n)=log(1+floor(n/p_i)), z_i in C; fixed prime-frame projector P[a][b]=cos(2pi*a*b/p_{m+1}) to R^3. Inputs n (scalar) and prime p_i; body returns a deterministic real 3D point = coord.

accepted.name: sqrt-p Q-independence distance-uniqueness
accepted.note: sqrt-p Q-independence witness: axis d at omega_d=sqrt(p_d); squared distance = sum p_i*delta_i^2 (integer combo of primes), guaranteeing distance-uniqueness. Inputs a coord (the pair of positions / digit deltas) and the prime-indexed radii; body returns the squared distance as a scalar.

accepted.name: Distinct-Distance Tower Lattice (DDTL) stride
accepted.note: DDTL stride: LEVEL_STRIDE(L)=P_L*3072, routing each line through the n*p*n^5 quintic seat so each line's distance carries a quintic prime signature. Inputs level L (scalar) and the level's distinct prime P_L; body returns the stride as a scalar added to the line-watcher distance.

accepted.name: hbi byte-offset sidecar
accepted.note: .hbi byte-offset sidecar: row=N|pid|bytes|sha256; to fetch a row seek to its indexed byte length and verify the sha256. Input is the pid (row key); body returns the row's raw bytes plus the sha256 used to verify (cost = index-lookup + one seek).

accepted.name: AMAZING NEW QUANT SERIES
accepted.note: AMAZING NEW QUANT SERIES (quant-series formula): from a quant sketch over a set of measured values, emits the append-only Q-vector [Q_gap (Steinhaus 3/2-gap), Q_cent, Q_resid, Q_edge (128-edge fingerprint)] plus the genius/mistake ratio scalar rho=2.50; Q_resid spikes at an undeclared prime -> new dim D48 (prime out).

accepted.name: 100B three-level prism reduction
accepted.note: 100B three-level prism reduction: raw harvest bytes (L0 19.3MB) -> L1 BEHCS-256 quant8 tuple (sketch, 6024x) -> L2 BEHCS-1024 sha512 fold (hash, 64B) -> L3 HyperBEHCS 10B cube glyph (referential content-address, raw->cube=1,927,778x scalar). Honest: glyph indexes its harvest, not lossless.

accepted.name: UCB1 exploration bonus
accepted.note: UCB1 exploration bonus = sqrt(2 * ln(N) / n_arm). Inputs: total pulls N (scalar), per-arm pulls n_arm (scalar). Output: exploration-bonus scalar added to per-arm reward for bandit arm selection over the arm_registry set.

accepted.name: MAP-Elites grid
accepted.note: MAP-Elites grid (quality-diversity algorithm): maps a behavior-descriptor vector (k-dim discretized axes e.g. cost/hyp_space/feedback) plus a fitness scalar into a behavior_grid, retaining the best-fitness elite per cell. Output: the set of elites (filled grid cells).

accepted.name: 3-axis Ltask Pareto-front
accepted.note: 3-axis Ltask Pareto-front (pareto formula): over a set of candidates each scored on the [cost, hyp_space, feedback] axis vector, selects the non-dominated set across all 3 axes. Output: the Pareto-optimal candidate set.

accepted.name: deep-wave address-cell count
accepted.note: deep-wave address-cell count (address-grid formula): given the dimension set (6 x 6 x 6 x 6 x 6 x 12), enumerates the deterministic address-cell set and its count scalar (=46656*... product). Cells are address slots gated by sealSha256/route-proofs, NOT immediate spawns.

accepted.name: answer-producer glyph hash
accepted.note: answer-producer glyph hash (glyph-address formula): glyph_full = sha256 over 0x1F-joined fixed fields {organ=APROD, ver, address_index (bh_index), lane, source_row_sha16 (hash), summary_sha16 (hash)} -> outputs glyph_full hash and the HG256:APROD_CANDIDATE first-8-hex glyph (routing hint, NOT identity).

accepted.name: omnitranslator sha16 glyph
accepted.note: omnitranslator sha16 glyph (glyph-address formula): sha256(text).digest('hex').slice(0,16). Input: text string (name). Output: 16-hex content-address hash used as a glyph/PID-style routing key.

accepted.name: hilbertAddress (6-glyph base-1024)
accepted.note: hilbertAddress / encodeGlyph (glyph-address function): encodes a uint64 value (scalar) into a width-6 base-1024 glyph string by taking 10-bit chunks against the 1024-glyph alphabet (GLYPHS[v & 1023]). decodeGlyph inverts glyph->scalar. Output: the 6-glyph base-1024 address.

accepted.name: agent-spawn throughput tier-F
accepted.note: agent-spawn throughput tier-F (throughput-measured formula): tier=F=write-sha256-sidecar-batched (batched persistence + sidecar hash per spawn over the spawn set); canonical acer measurement = 0.45M spawns/sec scalar. Note: the 1.73M figure was sha-bound and NOT an acer measurement.

accepted.name: Integer hash mix (Murmur-style 2x multiply-xorshift)
accepted.note: mix(x): Murmur-style 2x multiply-xorshift integer mixer. One numeric input (scalar), returns a >>>0 mixed 32-bit digest = hash. Pure, no deps.

accepted.name: Birthday-bound expected collisions (exact form)
accepted.note: expColl(N, bits): exact birthday-bound expected collisions. Two scalar inputs (N count, bits width); returns one scalar (expected collisions). Pure math.

accepted.name: Birthday-bound asymptotic (huge-S regime)
accepted.note: expCollBig(N, log2S): asymptotic N^2/(2S) birthday regime. Two scalar inputs (N, log2S); returns one scalar. Same family as 116055aa4b8bf5e8 but no functional dep (independent formula).

accepted.name: NDJSON-loose record parser
accepted.note: readJsonLoose(file): one input is the file path (name/identifier). Reads bytes, parses JSON object/array/NDJSON, and returns an array of records = a set. Arity 1, output set.

accepted.name: S-LoRA unified-paging page size H
accepted.note: S-LoRA unified-paging page size H: descriptive sizing parameter, not a function. BODY defines the page size as H elements = a single scalar dimension. No inputs, arity 0, output scalar.

accepted.name: MLC 100% human-level reasoning match claim
accepted.note: MLC 100% human-level reasoning match claim: kind=claim/result. BODY asserts a single result value (100% match to human-level systemic reasoning) = one scalar. No inputs, arity 0. Claim is UNVERIFIED text, but typed structurally as scalar result.

accepted.name: deterministicUnit (Mulberry32)
accepted.note: deterministicUnit (Mulberry32): pure PRNG over (seed,step) two integer scalars; returns a unit float in [0,1) — a scalar. No Math.random, no fabric inputs.

accepted.name: quant fill LCG (Numerical Recipes)
accepted.note: quant fill LCG (Numerical Recipes): fills the array arr (vector) in place from integer seed (scalar) via 1664525/1013904223 LCG, writing values in [-1,1). Mutates and yields the vector.

accepted.name: Mulberry32 deterministicUnit PRNG
accepted.note: Mulberry32 deterministicUnit PRNG (algorithm): identical body to c9679f08 — (seed,step) two scalars -> unit float scalar in [0,1).

accepted.name: classifyBhIndex (lane + ppow)
accepted.note: classifyBhIndex: takes a Brown-Hilbert linear index, returns lane (index%3) and ppow class string ('prime'/'p2'/'p3'/'pk'/'composite'/'unit') as a name. Domain 0..999999.

accepted.name: zetaClassify cylinder
accepted.note: zetaClassify cylinder: bh_index -> lane, ppow/prime_residence (name), residue6 (scalar), and cylinder {ring,phase} (coord). Delegates lane+ppow to classifyBhIndex (dep bf0f7eb9ef619939).

accepted.name: bhIndexLinear + Sidon prime-cube embedding
accepted.note: bhIndexLinear + Sidon prime-cube embedding: reg(name) hashes a name to sector/lane/glyph, then bhIndexLinear/bhIndexSidon fold those into a single linear bh_index using distinct prime-cube weights. Primary input name, output bh_index.

accepted.name: mintPid PID-division law
accepted.note: mintPid PID-division law: takes the spec name (role/tier/nest/kind around it) and sha256-mints a pid plus lane (seed%3), sector (seed%113), glyph (seed%1024) and sha16 hash; cube_bh is the sector.lane.glyph address. One object arg -> arity 1.

accepted.name: quant8 8-stage bucket encoders
accepted.note: quant8 8-stage bucket encoders: loops over projected vector proj, emitting turbo/signs/zeta/triple/quad/hist/von-Mangoldt (PPOW prime weights) channels — the multi-stage quant sketch. Input vector, output sketch.

accepted.name: dotQ quantized cosine numerator
accepted.note: dotQ quantized cosine numerator: two quant sketches qa,qb -> scalar (sum of turbo[j] products scaled by qa.scale*qb.scale/16129), the cosine numerator.

accepted.name: super-increasing cross-tower anchor
accepted.note: super-increasing cross-tower anchor: iterates the towers set, assigns each tower a super-increasing anchor (acc += (span+1)*BIG) and expands its difference set into the all set so towers' difference sets stay disjoint. Input set -> output set.

accepted.name: HRM applyShapePull manifold pulls
accepted.note: applyShapePull: pulls a candidate BEHCS-1024 cp (glyph idx) toward a shape-S manifold named by `shape`, biased by iter/totalIters scalars; clamps to [2,1023] -> bounded glyph.

accepted.name: frozen-gemma stub vector normalize
accepted.note: frozen-gemma stub normalize: maps raw seed bytes (seed[i%32]) into a normalized [-1,1] embedding-vector element. bytes -> vector (slice-stub).

accepted.name: rotateGnn — GNN-scored best-citizen pick
accepted.note: gnnScoreCitizen: builds 2 node vectors from sha16(intentHash) and sha16(citizenCp glyph), POSTs a 1-edge graph to GNN port (scalar), returns score scalar (0.5 fallback).

accepted.name: nu_lambda_invariant_measure
accepted.note: nu_lambda_invariant_measure: nuLambda(cp) maps a BEHCS cp (glyph idx>=2) to invariant measure 1/(cp*ln cp) scalar; companion bandWeight sums it over a band.

accepted.name: chainAntichainBound — Tao Prop 7 duality
accepted.note: chainAntichainBound: Tao Prop-7 chain/antichain duality. nConcurrent scalar -> required_mass_per_pid = 1/n (scalar) plus theorem/constructive-answer/citation name strings.

accepted.name: PIDChainRevolver.next — per-request rotor
accepted.note: PIDChainRevolver.next(): no args, reads internal counter; mints from actor(alphabet),lane(LANE_CYCLE),prime(primeAt); BODY returns the minted pid. arity=0.

accepted.name: prime-sector allocator (1M rooms/sector)
accepted.note: sectorFor(sectorIndex): scalar index in; BODY returns sector record with canonical prime, sectorId name, cp/glyph/coord64/tuple coord, chief name. 1M-room sector allocator.

accepted.name: roomCoords — 64-dim coordinate + cp + tuple from sha256
accepted.note: roomCoords(pid): pid in; sha256(pid)=64-hex coord64 (coord, 64 dims), cp scalar mod 1024, BEHCS-1024 glyph token, 6-axis tuple. BODY returns {coord64,cp,glyph,tuple}.

accepted.name: proximity graph — sha16 Hamming similarity
accepted.note: pidSimilarity(pidA,pidB) over hammingDistance: two sha16 pids in; nibble-popcount XOR Hamming distance → similarity scalar in [0,1]. arity=2.

accepted.name: Elo tournament (Co-Scientist Ranking §3.3.3)
accepted.note: expectedScore(eloA,eloB): two finite Elo scalars in; logistic 1/(1+10^((eloB-eloA)/400)) → expected-score scalar. Co-Scientist Elo tournament core. arity=2.

accepted.name: asolaria-score — 7-GNN composite + reverse-gain gate
accepted.note: score(pid,content): pid + content bytes in; sha16-derived node/edge feats fed to 7-GNN ensemble (L0:4792,L4:4793,G1-G4:4949) → signals vector + reverse-gain composite scalar.

accepted.name: omnishannon entropy signal (12-bin)
accepted.note: shannonSignal(content): content bytes in; 12-bin byte histogram → Shannon entropy H, normalized efficiency H/log2(12)=score signal. BODY returns {H,score,parts} scalars.

accepted.name: prime-cylinder / Hilbert tower (von Mangoldt visualization)
accepted.note: prime-cylinder/Hilbert tower: sieves primes up to N_MAX (scalar), enumerates prime powers p^k (prime + scalar k) and von Mangoldt lambda=ln p (scalar); outputs the prime set and per-power scalars for tower viz.

accepted.name: huge-message stress: bounded residency at 40GB
accepted.note: huge-message stress measurement: feeds 40GB message bytes, emits the fixed tuple_bytes=3200 quant sketch, raw_sha256/tuple_sha16 hashes, and bounded-residency scalars (peak_rss, resident_ratio, throughput).

accepted.name: LCG PID-emit (Lehmer / Knuth-Numerical-Recipes constants)
accepted.note: LCG PID-emit step: s = (imul(s,1664525)+1013904223)>>>0; advances the Lehmer/Knuth LCG state scalar to the next scalar.

accepted.name: LCG seed init (Knuth multiplicative + golden-ratio XOR)
accepted.note: LCG seed init: s = (imul((c+1),2654435761) ^ 0x9e3779b9)>>>0; folds counter scalar c with Knuth multiplier + golden-ratio constant into the seed scalar.

accepted.name: Packet score normalization
accepted.note: packet score normalization: score = s / 4294967295; maps the uint32 LCG state scalar to a [0,1] scalar.

accepted.name: Rule-of-3 lane separation
accepted.note: rule-of-3 lane separation: from counter c (scalar) derives seedId = SEEDS[c%len].split(':')[0] (name) and lane = c%3 (lane 0-2).

accepted.name: Count-sketch projection head (FNV-style hash bucket + sign)
accepted.note: count-sketch projection head: reads f64 source bytes, hashes each index h=(i*2654435761)>>>0, accumulates signed value into proj[h & (D-1)] producing the projected vector / count-sketch.

accepted.name: Read chunk byte-to-f64 stream loop
accepted.note: Streams 64MB byte chunks, reinterprets as Float64Array, folds each f64 (sign from index hash) into a fixed-D projection accumulator; body returns the proj vector.

accepted.name: classifyBhIndex_lane_ppow
accepted.note: classifyBhIndex: takes a Brown-Hilbert linear index, returns lane = index%3 (rule-of-three) and ppow class as a name string ('unit'/'prime'/'composite'/'p2'/'p3'/'pk').

accepted.name: rule-of-three lane via sha256 uint32 mod 3
accepted.note: laneFor: sha256 of the seed name, takes top 8 hex as uint32, returns uint32 % 3 = the rule-of-three lane (hash used internally on the name).

accepted.name: vector_quants_polar_turbo_jl_triple
accepted.note: Johnson-Lindenstrauss (Achlioptas sparse) + turbo + polar quant of an input vector; each returns quant codes/projection (sketch) plus a sha16 fingerprint (hash).

accepted.name: quadruple_fusion_fingerprint
accepted.note: quadrupleFingerprint: runs polar/turbo/JL/triple quant on the vector and returns a fused sha16 fingerprint over all four sketches plus a zeta band weight; body returns the combined hash.

accepted.name: Brown-Hilbert linear render scalar (bh_index / bhIndexLinear)
accepted.note: bhIndexLinear: linear Brown-Hilbert render scalar = sector*(WIDTH*3) + lane*WIDTH + glyph; companion reg(name) supplies the sector/lane/glyph triple.

accepted.name: Sidon prime-cube weighted embedding (bhIndexSidon)
accepted.note: bhIndexSidon: weights sector/lane/glyph each by a DISTINCT prime cube (13^3, 31^3, 73^3) so channel contributions are linearly independent over the integers; returns the Sidon bh_index.

accepted.name: Room/agent scalar address (roomAddress)
accepted.note: roomAddress: from node ordinal n derive level=floor(sqrt(n)), rightOffset, 47D dim selector, prime-scaled stride, returning coordinate = dim.cube + level + stride (scalar address; prime used internally as separator).

accepted.name: Node scalar Brown-Hilbert position with golden stride
accepted.note: Node Brown-Hilbert position: pos(NODE) = H_60(catalog-value vector) + A_tier(n)*STRIDE (mod 2^W), where A_tier(n) is the prime/golden-stride de-aliasing term keyed by ordinal n; returns the scalar bh position.

accepted.name: Tier non-collision proof (fundamental theorem of arithmetic)
accepted.note: Tier non-collision proof via fundamental theorem of arithmetic: two tower addresses (bh_index) collide only if their tower primes, exponents and n all match. Given two addresses and the distinguishing primes, returns the separation verdict (scalar boolean / prime-separated). Depends on the STE band construction it proves.

accepted.name: Single-cylinder pigeonhole readability ceiling
accepted.note: Single-cylinder pigeonhole readability ceiling: given slice size N (scalar) and render width W=SECTORS*WIDTH*3 (scalar), a distance collision is forced once C(N,2)>W i.e. N >= sqrt(2W). Returns the ceiling N* (scalar) / collision-forced verdict.

accepted.name: Cylinder Growth Law C(n)
accepted.note: Cylinder Growth Law C(n)=min{T : sum_{t<=T} p_t >= n} over the prime-cube anchor sequence (set of primes p_t), each absorbing ~p_t Sidon points. Given slice size n (scalar) and the prime sequence, returns required cylinder count T (scalar) ~ sqrt(2n/ln n). Builds on the per-cylinder sqrt ceiling.

accepted.name: M2 rolling projection licence predicate
accepted.note: M2 rolling projection licence predicate: maintains append-only distance signature set, compares running slice size N (scalar) to active cylinder count T (scalar) against C(N). Emits QUANTDELTA-ROLL with licence verdict (scalar HELD/LAPSED) and the named roll event. Depends on the growth law C(N) and the line-watcher signature set.

accepted.name: Regularized optimizer energy E(pi)
accepted.note: Regularized optimizer energy E(pi)=sum w_AB*d_pi(A,B) over slice pairs (set of weighted pipes) + churn/cut/hotspot penalties, evaluated under candidate placement pi (vector of cube coords). Returns total energy (scalar). Depends on the projection metric for d_pi(A,B).

accepted.name: Projection metric Phi and chord distance
accepted.note: Projection metric Phi(A)=anchor(tower_A)+sum_d s_d(A)*omega_d with omega_d=sqrt(p_d) and phase theta_d=2pi*frac(index*sqrt(p_d)). Maps an address's per-dimension coordinate (coord) plus dimension primes to its projected position; chord distance d=||Phi(A)-Phi(B)||. Returns the projected coord. Uses the STE tower anchors.

accepted.name: Line/pipe signature (mlc-line-watcher)
accepted.note: Line/pipe signature (mlc-line-watcher lineBetween): distance=|b.bh_index - a.bh_index| (scalar) and signature=sha16([a.pid,b.pid,bh_points,stride,distance,bucket,relation,...]) (hash). Given the two endpoints' linear indices, returns the line distance and its signature.

accepted.name: Three named laws (M_fabric / slice / tail-O(1))
accepted.note: Three named fabric laws. M_fabric: addr(e)=(H(PID(e)),t(e)) injective, read(a) is O(1) recomputation (loss prob ~2^-256). Slice law S_next=E(S_now,Delta), E=0 => frozen. Tail-O(1): E2E=HEAD+sum TAIL ~ HEAD. Given an event pid and the address hash function, yields the address hash and O(1) read cost (scalar). Depends on the PID identity scheme.

accepted.name: Brown Gap Series / BHGS readout + Brown bend
accepted.note: Brown Gap Series (BHGS) readout: takes the set of certified-distinct distance gaps and a genius-minus-mistake reverse-gain scalar weighting, emits the weighted readout series (vector) and the Brown bend beta_D (scalar) prime-pattern. Well-defined iff uniqueness holds; head ~3/7 on 100B totals.

accepted.name: sha256 primary hash
accepted.note: sha256 primary hash: String(value) coerced to bytes -> full sha256 hex digest. Root hash primitive for the whole quant/PID stack.

accepted.name: sha16 truncated handle
accepted.note: sha16 truncated handle: stringifies value to bytes, calls sha256, slices first 16 hex. Produces the 16-hex handle used as fingerprint/PID-shaped digest. Depends on sha256.

accepted.name: Achlioptas sparse random projection weight
accepted.note: Achlioptas sparse random projection weight: hashes seed|row|col via sha256, takes mod-6 bucket -> returns +sqrt(3), -sqrt(3), or 0 weight scalar. Deterministic sparse projection matrix entry; depends on sha256.

accepted.name: Johnson-Lindenstrauss projection
accepted.note: Johnson-Lindenstrauss projection: normalizes input vector, draws each matrix entry from achlioptasWeight(seed,...), scales by 1/sqrt(targetDim), returns the projected vector plus sha16 fingerprint hash. arity 2 = (vector, seed name in options).

accepted.name: turbo uniform scalar quant
accepted.note: Turbo uniform scalar quant: min/max normalize the vector into integer codes at 'bits' resolution, returns the quant sketch (codes + metadata scale/min/max) and a sha16 fingerprint hash. arity 2 = (vector, bits scalar in options).

accepted.name: polar pair quant
accepted.note: Polar pair quant: pairs consecutive components into (radius,angle), quantizes radius by maxRadius/levels and angle over 2pi into integer code pairs, returns the polar sketch plus sha16 fingerprint hash. arity 2 = (vector, bits scalar in options).

accepted.name: triple spherical quant
accepted.note: Triple spherical quant: groups components into (radius,theta,phi) triples, quantizes each into a 3-lane code (coarse/medium/fine lanes), returns the spherical sketch with explicit lanes array. arity 2 = (vector, bits scalar in options); also emits sha16 fingerprint.

accepted.name: quantBits clamp
accepted.note: quantBits clamp (plus round/finiteNumber helpers): coerces a value to a finite number and clamps the bit-depth scalar into [1,24]. Pure scalar->scalar guard used by all quant formulas.

accepted.name: triple-quant reconstruction (inverse)
accepted.note: Triple-quant reconstruction (inverse of tripleQuant): reads the sketch's code triples + metadata scales (radius/theta/phi), maps each back through spherical->cartesian, returns the reconstructed coordinate vector trimmed to packet.dimension. Depends on round/finiteNumber.

accepted.name: vectorFromText deterministic embedding
accepted.note: vectorFromText: hashes a text/name string into utf8 bytes, then derives a fixed-dimension deterministic embedding vector by indexing those bytes; returns the vector.

accepted.name: ZERO_COUNTERS E=0 invariant set
accepted.note: ZERO_COUNTERS: a frozen constant set of E=0 invariant counters (all zero); pure declaration, no inputs, emits the counter set.

accepted.name: 100B global PID namespace
accepted.note: 100B global PID namespace constant: declares a pid set spanning fromPid..toPid (1e11 pids) plus a preloadHash digest; no inputs, emits the pid set and hash.

accepted.name: HRM slow-fast nested trajectory (no-grad) + 1-step grad
accepted.note: HRM slow-fast nested trajectory: takes hidden-state vectors z_H, z_L and input_embeddings vector, runs no-grad nested H/L cycles plus a 1-step grad update, returns refined z_H and z_L vectors.

accepted.name: HRM puzzle-embedding ceil-div length
accepted.note: ceil-div: puzzle_emb_len = -(puzzle_emb_ndim // -hidden_size); two scalar config dims in, one scalar length out.

accepted.name: Q-head zero/-5 bias init
accepted.note: Weight-init: q_head.weight.zero_() and bias.fill_(-5); takes the q-head param vector and returns it initialized (zero weights, -5 bias).

accepted.name: CastedLinear LeCun init
accepted.note: LeCun trunc-normal init: std=1/sqrt(in_features); out_features and in_features scalars in, an (out,in) weight vector out.

accepted.name: Stablemax (numerically stable softmax surrogate)
accepted.note: Stablemax activation s(x): elementwise stable softmax surrogate over logit vector x; one vector in, normalized log-prob vector out.

accepted.name: ACT total loss combination
accepted.note: ACT total loss: lm_loss + 0.5*(q_halt_loss + q_continue_loss); three scalar loss terms in, one scalar total out. Consumes stablemax lm loss and bootstrap target-Q loss.

accepted.name: RMSNorm
accepted.note: RMSNorm: hidden_states * rsqrt(mean(square)+variance_epsilon); hidden-state vector + epsilon scalar in, normalized vector out.

accepted.name: Sparse-embedding SignSGD with decoupled weight decay
accepted.note: Sparse-embedding SignSGD: all-gather + unique-scatter grad, p.mul_(1-lr*wd).add_(sign(grad),-lr); grad vector, ids set, weights vector, lr/weight_decay/world_size scalars in, updated weights vector out.

accepted.name: HRM absorption Shannon symbol bits
accepted.note: Shannon-bits: map a symbol(name) and its count(value scalar) to bits=ceil(log2(value)) (scalar) plus a 16-hex fingerprint (hash). Body emits {symbol,value,bits,fingerprint} per row.

accepted.name: HRM descriptor-only absorption fingerprint + commit
accepted.note: Absorption-descriptor: over a set of HRM source artifacts, emit a git commit (hash) and a counts table (set of metric scalars, all runtime/mutation counts = 0). Descriptor-only, E=0.

accepted.name: HRM source-hash readback manifest (sha256)
accepted.note: Integrity-hash readback: given a file path(name) and its raw bytes, compute sha256 (hash), byte length (scalar), and a 16-hex fingerprint, with readback_ok flag. All mutation counters 0.

accepted.name: Tool-citizen 4D/4-bit Hilbert coord from PID hash
accepted.note: BEHCS Hilbert mint: sha256(pid) -> first 4 bytes & 0x0f -> 4D/4-bit Hilbert coord, then hilbertEncode -> linear bh_index (voxId hex). Pure pid->geometry address.

accepted.name: Room-collision birthday + chi-squared uniformity
accepted.note: Room-collision stats: over a set (room histogram of fired pids) and fired count N(scalar), compute chi-squared vs uniform (df9 crit 16.919), skew sigma ratio, and birthday expected collisions -- all scalar outputs.

accepted.name: Cube-coverage envelope row_hash + 47D cube address
accepted.note: Cube-coverage envelope: row_hash = sha256('FEDENV|'+caller_pid+verb+payload+ts).slice16 (hash); also stamps a 47D cube address string cube_47d (name) with glyph_5 + cosign token. verb=name, payload=bytes.

accepted.name: Glyph-5 HBP roundtrip preservation probe
accepted.note: Glyph-5 HBP roundtrip probe: encodeHbp then decodeHbp an adversarial glyph_5; compare utf8 hex (bytes) of original vs recovered to yield preserved boolean (scalar). Encoding fidelity test.

accepted.name: District supervisor formats + band
accepted.note: District-supervisor descriptor: from district entity(name) emit a Brown-Hilbert supervisor_pid (pid), band cp-704 (sector), room_count and a row_hash (hash). HBPv1 descriptor row, json=0 runtime=0.

accepted.name: Hilbert curve order-6 voxel substrate
accepted.note: Hilbert-substrate manifest: order=6 (scalar) defines a 64^3=262144-voxel substrate (scalar count) addressed by the brown-hilbert PID formula = linear bh_index over the curve.

accepted.name: 3D map brown-hilbert projection
accepted.note: to3DMapPoint: brown-hilbert projection maps a linear Brown-Hilbert index onto a 3D x/y/z voxel coord (axes=x_y_z, live_voxels=94).

accepted.name: BEHCS-256/1024 subset-embedding registry law
accepted.note: BEHCS subset-embedding law: lifts a BEHCS-256 glyph index into the BEHCS-1024 space (first 256 identical), per behcs.mjs.

accepted.name: Auto-translate language stack
accepted.note: autoTranslate: emits the set of multi-language reps (BEHCS-256 + BEHCS-1024 glyphs + sha16 hash + glyph-5-apex) for one identifier; canonical=true.

accepted.name: Universal-route dual-emit + voxel band
accepted.note: Universal-route dual-emit: for a destination pid emits the set {destination-route, hookwall-observation}, both banded into hilbert voxels 694-697 (chain_seq=243).

accepted.name: Session-ops cosign receipt row (10000-room rotor)
accepted.note: Session-ops cosign receipt: keys a session pid (carries bh_index=3059, tuple) to a sealed row_hash (8bb52013147325b3); descriptor-only, zero-authority.

accepted.name: GNN absorption edge schema (descriptor, zero-authority)
accepted.note: GNN absorption edge schema: from-node name + to-node name (relation) reduce to an edge fingerprint hash; grants_authority=false, all mutation counters 0.

accepted.name: Singer planar difference set (N,k,1) construction
accepted.note: planarDifferenceSet(q): from prime-power scalar q builds a Singer planar (N=q^2+q+1, k=q+1, 1) difference set D (the returned set, translation-invariant first element 0).

accepted.name: All-differences-distinct proof
accepted.note: allDifferencesDistinct({N,D}): given modulus scalar N and difference set D, returns boolean scalar that all N-1 ordered differences are distinct and nonzero (verifies the Singer set).

accepted.name: Invertible difference ledger O(1) resolve
accepted.note: differenceLedger({N,D}): builds invertible Sidon difference map over modulus N (scalar) and difference set D (set). Returns distance(i,j)=mod(D[j]-D[i],N) -> exact integer scalar address, and resolve(delta)=inv.get(delta) -> O(1) back to [i,j] index pair (set).

accepted.name: Prime-cylinder display coordinate
accepted.note: cylinderCoord(diffSet): takes the Sidon diffSet {N,D} (set) and returns a node->{x,y,z} coord function. Maps node id via sha256 room hash into D[r], theta=2*pi*D[r]/N around a prime-cylinder, height from seq. Output is a 3D coord. Internally uses hash and the difference-ledger structure.

accepted.name: Voxel default coordinate layout
accepted.note: defaultCoord(node): takes a node identifier/name and returns {x,y,z} coord. x = agentLane = (sha256 hashInt('lane:'+id) % LANES) * LANE_SPACING (a lane bucket projected to x), y = kindY from kind hash, z = seq. Hash-driven lane/layer layout -> coord.

accepted.name: 1e200 virtual symbolic blast primitive
accepted.note: 1e200 virtual symbolic blast primitive: reads the architecture map rows (set of bytes), per row computes sha256 hash, addr=hash mod 1e200 (huge scalar symbolic address), band=addr mod 57 Singer (57,8,1) cylinder band (sector-like collision band), cube=3 bytes mod 8 (3D coord), kind tag. Returns a set of nodes each carrying band+cube+kind.

accepted.name: 1e200 blast result artifact
accepted.note: 1e200 blast result artifact (kind=constant): the stored HBPv1 record (raw bytes) of the 1e200 blast run -- total_symbolic_agents=1e200, primitive=sha-PID-mint-mod-1e200-then-Singer-57-band, nodes=94, collision_bands=26, genius=28, mistake_open=24, plus COLLISION rows listing band/count/nodes. No inputs; it is a deterministic produced artifact (bytes).

accepted.name: FNV-1a lexical hashing embedder (signed)
accepted.note: createEmbedder returns embed(text): tokenizes a name/text string, fnv1a-hashes each token, bucket=h%dim, sign from top bit, accumulates into a dim=256 array then L2-normalizes. Output is an embedding vector. Signed FNV-1a lexical hashing embedder (hash-based).

accepted.name: Local MiniLM embedder
accepted.note: Local MiniLM embedder (kind=constant factory): createLocalEmbedder returns {id, dim:384, embed}. embed(text) runs the Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 feature-extraction pipeline with mean pooling + normalize and returns a length-384 L2-normalized embedding vector. name/text in -> vector out.

accepted.name: Vector durable codec (base64 float32)
accepted.note: Vector durable codec: encodeVec(arr) packs a numeric vector into a Float32Array buffer and base64-encodes it to durable bytes; decodeVec(b64) is the inverse, reconstructing the vector from the bytes. Primary direction encode: vector -> base64 bytes (Float32 little-endian).

accepted.name: HBI sidecar index row
accepted.note: HBI sidecar index row (kind=schema): defines the HBIv1 sidecar row format -- each row carries row index, pid (e.g. ACER-REVOLVER-CHAMBER-NNNN), bytes=length, sha256=digest (hash), json=0/runtime=0/promote=0 flags. A structural schema describing the index-row fields, not a function.

accepted.name: mintPID federation identity sha16
accepted.note: mintPID(actor,{device,lane,prime}): federation identity minter. Joins actor name + device hostname (name) + lane + prime into `actor|dev|lane|prime`, sha256-hashes and slices first 16 hex -> a 16-hex pid. Hash-based identity mint; device defaults to os.hostname, lane and prime are the namespacing inputs alongside the actor name.

accepted.name: GNN civilization-edge weight
accepted.note: GNN civilization edge: maps a source pid to a destination agency (name) with an edge weight (scalar ~0.05). Body rows are GNN-EDGE|src=pid|dst=agency:NAME|weight=scalar.

accepted.name: Revolver capability gate vector (E=0 ledger)
accepted.note: Revolver capability gate: HBP row anchored to a pid, exposing the E=0 capability ledger as a vector of 0/1 gate flags (endpoint/usb_write/dispatch/...) sealed by row_hash.

accepted.name: Per-packet pipeline (1e200 engine) canon
accepted.note: Per-packet 1e200 pipeline: Hookwall -> fwd GNN -> reverse-gain GNN -> OmniShannon -> compacting GC over a packet (bytes); sha256 per stream = the proof hash, top marks glyphed into BEHCS-256/1024.

accepted.name: 100B run scale ladder + counts
accepted.note: 100B scale ladder constant: the set of tiers (1M -> 100b -> 1t -> 100t -> 1e27 -> 12e27 -> 1e200) plus fixed counts (targetPackets=100,000,000,000, 277.8M genius + 111.1M mistake hits). Named constant collection, no inputs.

accepted.name: Federation Kernel surface
accepted.note: Federation Kernel v0.2.0 surface: a fixed set of 16 syscalls / named modules (pid minter, envelope, crypto ed25519, hookwall, cosign_chain, agent_runtime, bus_fabric, vfs, tier_gate). Named constant surface, no runtime inputs.

accepted.name: bring-your-own-key identity mint
accepted.note: BYO-key identity mint: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f <name> -N '' produces the ed25519 keypair (bytes); private stays local, node.ed25519.pub is published as the node identity.

accepted.name: cylindrical prime-lattice theta
accepted.note: theta_i = 2*pi*log(p_i)/log(p_max=53): maps a prime to its angular scalar on the cylindrical prime-lattice.

accepted.name: cylindrical prime-lattice radius
accepted.note: r_k=k*unit_radius then x=r*cos(theta),y=r*sin(theta),z=i: ring-rank k, theta scalar, index i -> 3D coord. Depends on theta formula.

accepted.name: prime-power ring census
accepted.note: Constant ring census: 5 prime-power rings (16/12/10/8/6 primes) each with a hilbert (bh_index) band. Emits the prime sets and their bh_index ranges.

accepted.name: hilbert-hotel sub-PROF roster
accepted.note: Constant SUB_PROFS roster: named seats each at a 3D coord (+color scalar) plus GEODESIC pairs; emits a set of names with coords.

accepted.name: sovlinux init boot constants
accepted.note: SOVLINUX boot constants: path/scope name strings, port scalars (BUS 4947/HEALTH 4952, QUINTUPLE_NEED=5), and EXPECTED_MANIFEST_SHA256 hash.

accepted.name: health-loop degraded policy
accepted.note: Health loop: polls HEALTH_PORT, returns HTTP status scalar; PID-1 degraded policy after 5 fails. Consumes the boot port scalar.

accepted.name: quintuple-cosign signer check
accepted.note: Quintuple check: reads cosign.ndjson bytes, counts "signer" matches in the last scope row, returns signer-count scalar gated >= QUINTUPLE_NEED=5.

accepted.name: image-tree merkle seal
accepted.note: Image-tree merkle seal: sha256 over IMAGE_TREE_MANIFEST bytes -> the 927dc0a4.. digest hash.

accepted.name: capacity ledgers (address space, NOT live)
accepted.note: capacity-constant: premade address-space ledgers as constants — 100B packet PIDs + 10B human-PID (minted not spawned), route capacity 10,000 rooms x 7 lanes = 70,000, live agents = 0. No inputs; emits the scalar capacity numbers as a set of constants.

accepted.name: phone-connector promotion state machine
accepted.note: state-machine: phone-connector promotion lattice (buildPhoneConnectorLattice/Bootstrap). Takes a set of readiness facts + lane rows, ANDs gateReady/executionReady/etc, emits typed_lanes status rows (set of lane-id:status names).

accepted.name: GLYPH-GENESIS 8-glyph BEHCS-256 minter
accepted.note: algorithm: GLYPH-GENESIS BEHCS-256 minter. glyph(key,alphabet): hashes key (name) via sha256, reads first 8 bytes big-endian u64, base-256 expands against the alphabet (set) into an 8-glyph string. Outputs a glyph.

accepted.name: measured quant ratio table (CALIBRATION_ROWS)
accepted.note: ratio table: CALIBRATION_ROWS constant — measured quant sketch benchmark rows (sha_gain/write_gain/compare_gain scalars across 1MB..2048MB payloads). No inputs; a fixed set of measured scalar ratios over the quant sketch.

accepted.name: head encode-rate flatness (linear head tax)
accepted.note: law: head encode-rate flatness — constant assertion that head-encode throughput is flat 1.8-2.1 GBps from 256MB to 2GB (head tax exactly linear, never degrades). No inputs; states a scalar-rate invariant.

accepted.name: omniScore sha-modular score
accepted.note: formula: omniScore(k). k=name; sha256(name), takes first 4 hex chars, parseInt base 16, mod 1001 -> a scalar score in [0,1000].

accepted.name: quant fidelity honest bound (adversarial break)
accepted.note: law: quant fidelity honest bound. States cosine error ~0.001-0.033 (scalar) on dense/sparse vectors but breaks on adversarial-cancel families and exact bucket collisions — so the sketch tuple is only an approximate routing index; pair a raw hash for exact identity. No inputs; asserts a scalar fidelity bound.

accepted.name: cross-vantage invariant convergence (SHIPPED==REIMPL==LIRIS)
accepted.note: law: cross-vantage invariant convergence. Compares reimpl/shipped/liris captured results — sketch tuple bytes (sha16 hash + byte-identical equals), zeta forcing pairs, quant4 collisions, extreme mod3/mod6 — and emits CONVERGE/DIVERGE verdict rows. Reads captured globals (no formal params); outputs a set of verdict rows.

accepted.name: sha16_of (first-8-bytes sha256, 16 hex)
accepted.note: cosign-hash: sha16_of(bytes). sha256 over raw bytes, takes first 8 bytes, renders 16 hex chars -> the 16-hex hash/PID digest.

accepted.name: shannonVerdict L0-L6 score
accepted.note: shannonVerdict: one envelope (set) -> canonical JSON -> sha256 hash -> extract 7 L0-L6 bits -> score = sum/7. Body returns {levels, score}; primary numeric product is the score scalar.

accepted.name: omniGnnScore (forward ensemble)
accepted.note: omniGnnScore({hookwall,shannon,whiteroom}): consumes hookwall.verdict (name->h_score), shannon.score (scalar from shannonVerdict 281), whiteroom.cube_address (coord from whiteroomCubeAddress 282); weighted ensemble 0.4h+0.35s+0.25w -> score scalar + decision name.

accepted.name: reverseGainGnnScore (mask sign-flip)
accepted.note: reverseGainGnnScore({hookwall,shannon,whiteroom,envelope}): hookwall.verdict (name), shannon.score (scalar), whiteroom.cube_address (coord), plus envelope (set) scanned for mask markers -> mask_flip sign; reverse-gain weighted score scalar + decision name.

accepted.name: gnnScore EWMA routing weight
accepted.note: gnnScore(_sha, plane, kind): _sha hash (deterministic seed slot, unused now), plane name, kind name -> keys plane|kind EWMA map -> returns learned routing weight in [0,1] = scalar (default-closed PRIOR_WEIGHT 0.5).

accepted.name: mineReverseGain (JL-magnitude * sign)
accepted.note: mineReverseGain(env): one env (set) -> vectorFromEnvelope + quantMetadata jl yields a vector, jlMagnitude = L2 norm scalar; glyph membership in MASK_GLYPHS sets sign +/-1; score = sign*dist. Body returns {score,mask_flipped,sign,glyph,jl_distance}; primary product is the signed score scalar.

accepted.name: mineEdge (edge-mining weight)
accepted.note: mineEdge helpers: one env (set) -> quantMetadata jl projection (sketch/vector) -> jlFingerprint reduces to L2-norm squashed scalar in [0,1]; edgeQuantTail packs JL bytes -> 8-hex tail (hash signature). Edge-mining weight is the scalar plus the quant-tail hash.

accepted.name: GLSM latent state machine (G4)
accepted.note: GLSM transition(prev_state, {edge_weight, path_confidence, reverse_gain}): prev_state latent-state name + three finite scalars; default-closed pure transition over frozen LATENT_STATES with TAU_PATH/TAU_GAIN cutoffs -> next latent-state name (CONVERGED absorbing).

accepted.name: fnv1a64 host_handle8 (8-byte host identity)
accepted.note: fnv1a64(input:&str)->u64 FNV-1a over canonicalized bytes; host_handle8 formats it as {:016x} = a 16-hex host identity = pid. Returns the 16-hex string (pid). Raw u64 is the hash stage but the composable output is the 8-byte host pid.

accepted.name: polar-pair quant (radius/angle codes)
accepted.note: polar-pair quant: (x,y) pairs (vector) + bits clamped [1,24] (scalar) -> per-pair [radius_code,angle_code] quant codes = a quant sketch. PolarMetadata records bits/max_radius.

accepted.name: triple-spherical quant (r/theta/phi codes)
