accepted.note: triple-spherical quant: (x,y,z) triples (vector) + bits (scalar) -> per-triple [r,theta,phi] codes = quant sketch. TripleMetadata holds radius/theta/phi scales.

accepted.name: sovlinux init-rust PID-1 boot (real sha256 gate)
accepted.note: sovlinux PID-1 boot gate: reads MANIFEST.hbp (bytes), computes Sha256 and compares to build-baked EXPECTED_MANIFEST_SHA256 (hash). Output is the boot/health gate decision (scalar pass-fail; PID-1 no-respawn on fail). Core formula = sha256(manifest)==expected.

accepted.name: Recurrence-is-mind corrective gate (depth-N prime nest)
accepted.note: Recurrence corrective gate, depth-7 (PRIME) branching-2 tree: build(tamperAddr) where addr is a name. Each node mints agent+watcher pids via sha16, recomputes truth=sha16('work|'+addr), gate_ok = reported==trueVal, subtree_ok rolls up. Returns the verified node tree (set of gated nodes) plus nodeCount.

accepted.name: depth-3 self-reflect tree (3^3 wave)
accepted.note: depth-3 branching-3 (3^3=27 leaf) self-reflect tree: runTree(tamperPath) where tamperPath is a node-path name. Leaves do truth(addr)=sha16 mod 1e6, watcher recomputes, gate_ok=reported==watcherTruth; supervisors authorize iff every child passed, roll up sha16. Returns the gated node tree (set) with failing-path list.

accepted.name: bhIndexSidon prime-cube embedding (Golomb idea)
accepted.note: bhIndexSidon prime-cube embedding (Golomb/Sidon idea): from a reg record r={sector,lane,glyph}, returns sector*p0^3 + lane*p3^3 + glyph*p6^3 using DISTINCT prime cubes (PRIMES[13,17,23,31,41,47,73,79,83,89,131]) so channels are linearly independent over the integers = a Sidon Brown-Hilbert linear index. Depends on prime cubes.

accepted.name: Atlas cp->domain/supervisor/lane 8-band partition
accepted.note: domain_for_cp / supervisor_for_cp / lane_for_cp: range-partition a glyph codepoint (0-1023) into 8 atlas bands -> domain+supervisor sector band and a lane. glyph -> sector,lane.

accepted.name: BEHCS-1024 PID format + parse/mint
accepted.note: parse_pid(pid): split the BEHCS-1024 PID grammar (role-name-PID-Hhex-A##-W###-P##-N#####) into structured parts; extracts role/name, activity/wave (sector-like), lane region, and prime block. pid -> name,sector,lane,prime.

accepted.name: slice-addresser 50D Brown-Hilbert -> Gemma layer/neuron slice
accepted.note: pidToSliceCoord(pid): sha256(pid) hashed into a 50D coord = 16x3 barycentric simplex weights + layer(0..25) + mtp_offset(0..2). hash->manifold address of a frozen Gemma slice.

accepted.name: GNN reverse-gain mask-flip scorer
accepted.note: mineReverseGain(env): reads env.glyph + the jl vector; score = sign(+1, or -1 if glyph in MASK_GLYPHS) * ||jl vector||. Signed-distance reverse-gain scalar.

accepted.name: supervisor-collision-router classify+route (5-state)
accepted.note: supervisor-collision-router: takes a set of colliding entities (each carrying a name/id via entityId and a bh_index via bandOf), classifies into 5 states (real-collision vs logical-preserved vs namespace-split), routes to a decision name, and emits an HBP pipe-row whose row_hash is sha16 over the prefix.

accepted.name: dispatch-hbp-lane HBP pipe-row emitter
accepted.note: dispatchHbpRow(env, kind): builds a DISPATCH{PENDING,FIRED,VETO} HBP wire-row from the dispatch envelope's pid, bh_addr (behcs1024 brown_hilbert_address), score scalar, and kind/action name; appends json=0 then row_hash=sha16(prefix). Same hbpRow grammar as the collision-router.

accepted.name: free-agent-fanout prime-slot model dispatch
accepted.note: spawnWorker(i): rotor index i selects prime=PRIMES[i%7], then model name=MODELS[prime%2], spawns the worker and returns the FREE_AGENT_OUTPUT envelope (set/payload). Scalar slot index in -> prime + model name + envelope set out.

accepted.name: GNN convergence-gate + reset-halve glyph math
accepted.note: GNN glyph-op family (RESET-HALVE toward 0.5, CONVERGE-GATE, REVERSE-GAIN, MASK-FLIP=-bias): takes a glyph operand plus its current scalar weight/bias and returns the updated scalar.

accepted.name: D11 proof-level lattice
accepted.note: D11 proof-level lattice: maps a claim identifier (name) to its evidence level (PROVEN/OBSERVED/INHERITED/ASSUMED) -- a name out. Pure taxonomy lookup over the levels set.

accepted.name: vonMangoldtNext divisor weights
accepted.note: vonMangoldtNext: divisor set divs + cp scalar + seed scalar + step scalar -> normalized Lambda(q)/log(cp) weights vector, then deterministic-unit pick returning next/divisor/weight (scalars) plus a reason name. Depends on vonMangoldt (4e7bdd1e).

accepted.name: DISPUTED Hilbert band 930-1229
accepted.note: Hilbert-collision canon: qualifies a bh_index by vantage name (ACER/LIRIS/SHARED) against DISPUTED_BANDS {lo:930,hi:1229}. Body exports the vantage set + disputed band set used to gate a bh_index binding; returns the disputed-band set membership. address-collision-canon kind.

accepted.name: scope escalation table
accepted.note: SCOPES table maps a scope name (read/attest/bind-draft/mint/write) to its escalates scalar (0 or 1). Lookup by name -> escalation scalar. auth-scope/safety-gate kind.

accepted.name: Polar pair quant (2D radius/angle)
accepted.note: polarQuant(vector, options.bits scalar): pairs values into radius/angle, quantizes to code pairs (the polar quant sketch) with metadata, plus sha16 fingerprint hash. quant-polar-pair formula.

accepted.name: Triple spherical quant (3D radius/theta/phi)
accepted.note: tripleQuant(vector, options.bits scalar): triples values into radius/theta/phi, quantizes to code triples (the quant sketch) with coarse/medium/fine lanes and metadata, plus sha16 fingerprint hash. quant-triple-spherical formula.

accepted.name: Hilbert curve d2xy/xy2d index (16-bit)
accepted.note: hilbertIndex (xy2d): maps two grid coordinates (x,y) + bits scalar to a 1D Brown-Hilbert linear index.

accepted.name: BEHCS-256 PID tuple (cube-of-cubes Hilbert)
accepted.note: BEHCS-256 PID tuple (actor,device,lane,prime): actor name + device sha256-12 hash + lane + prime, Hilbert-mapped to one 1D PID index; collide iff tuples equal.

accepted.name: quant HEAD-tax / TAIL-gain benchmark ladder (acer node v24.13.1)
accepted.note: quant HEAD-tax/TAIL-gain benchmark ladder: runs the 3.1KB quant sketch over message bytes (1MB..2GB) and emits a set of QUANTBENCHRESULT receipt rows (head_ms, sha_gain, write/compare gains).

accepted.name: HBP head digest
accepted.note: readHbpHead: reads file bytes, takes head-length scalar (default 4096), returns sha256_head (hash) over the leading slice plus size (scalar) and path (name). Body returns {path,size,sha256_head,preview_utf8}.

accepted.name: os-on-metal 7-layer fusion config
accepted.note: os-on-metal 7-layer fusion CFG: resolves env-var keys (name) to a set of named endpoint URLs/paths (fabric_url, hermes_store, anthropic/gemini urls, kernel roots, hookwall, cosign path). Body is a constant object literal => set of named layers.

accepted.name: PID = (actor, device, lane, prime) Hilbert-curve tuple
accepted.note: Bijective PID mint: (actor name, device name, lane, prime) -> pid as a Hilbert-curve tuple, zero collisions by construction; the lazy-materialized 100B PIDs index into a Brown-Hilbert linear bh_index. 61MB prime-list seed = Big-Pickle.

accepted.name: 10-byte SHA cube addressing
accepted.note: 10-byte SHA cube addressing / address-compression: hash agent-state bytes to a 10-byte cryptographic fingerprint (hash), then place it in Brown-Hilbert space as coord + linear bh_index. Generates ~10^200 structural paths via geometry, not live compute; 2TB USB maps billions of compact keys.

accepted.name: System summary metrics (2023 theory / 2026 impl)
accepted.note: system-throughput-summary: prose descriptor of the OS; nullary producer emitting fixed scalar metrics (100M agents, 39k/sec, 230M mistakes). No deterministic inputs; not a callable formula.

accepted.name: pidMap (hash -> room + hilbert)
accepted.note: pidMap: slices a payloadHash hex string into x,y (coord), room%10000 (sector), pid:slice(0,16); calls hilbertIndex(x,y) for the bh_index. Depends on the Hilbert index formula.

accepted.name: BEHCS-1024 supervisor band -> quant map
accepted.note: BEHCS-1024 supervisor band->quant atlas table: a cp linear position index (Brown-Hilbert/coordinate-position band) maps to a quant-scheme name + domain name + supervisor name (e.g. 256-383 -> triple/sovereignty/gaia). Lookup: bh_index -> name. arity=1. Static atlas, no callee deps.

accepted.name: Eight quant engines roster
accepted.note: Eight quant engines roster spec: Polar/Turbo/JL/Zeta/Triple/Quadruple/JS/von-Mangoldt. Body is a named collection of engines; given a roster name it enumerates the set of engine identifiers.

accepted.name: OMNI MFT-SCALE census totals
accepted.note: Static census-receipt literal: BODY is a JSON totals block returning grand_count=15020 and grand_bytes=5390074631 (two scalars) plus biggest/oldest file metadata. No function args, so arity 0; outputs are the two scalar totals the body holds.

accepted.name: HBP heartbeat vs per-agent token economy
accepted.note: HBP heartbeat vs per-agent token economy: BODY consumes projN (one scalar) with const BATCH=1e6 to compute heartbeatRows and the antipattern ratio. Outputs are scalar row-count and scalar ratio (the 'xfewer' factor) it pushes to the log.

accepted.name: Helm pipe + fabric endpoint topology
accepted.note: Fabric/helm endpoint topology: BODY is a static array literal of named endpoint URLs (acer-primary, liris-mirror, pool, omnispindle, helm-4920->4922->4924). No args; yields a set of endpoints each carrying a name identifier.

accepted.name: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with k=60
accepted.note: reciprocal_rank_fusion(search_results_dict, k=60): inputs are the per-query results collection (set) and the constant k (scalar). Returns reranked_results = a set ordered by fused scalar scores. Arity 2; outputs set + scalar (fused score).

accepted.name: Shannon engine beat counts
accepted.note: Shannon engine beat counts: kind=constant. Literal object {v4:2592, v5:93312} beats; nullary, emits fixed scalar counts. No composition inputs.

accepted.name: citizenIdentity — model-citizen PID/bh/glyph/cube_cell
accepted.note: citizenIdentity: from a citizen identifier derives MODEL-* pid (sha16 hash), glyph (sha16), cube_cell (sector cell), and bh_3d_idx via hilbertEncode (Hilbert nD encoder 149).

accepted.name: rotate — round-robin prism cursor
accepted.note: rotate: round-robin prism cursor over a citizen set (opts/census), picks next ready citizen for an intent name, spreads citizenIdentity (pid/glyph/cube_cell/bh) plus rotation_idx scalar.

accepted.name: BEHCS-256/1024 glyph alphabet (subset embedding)
accepted.note: BEHCS glyph alphabet: makeGlyphs(n) builds an n-length frozen set of cpXXXX glyph names; BEHCS256/1024 subset embeddings. scalar size -> set of glyphs.

accepted.name: Hilbert nD encode (Skilling 2004)
accepted.note: Hilbert nD encode (Skilling 2004): transposeToAxesND / axesToTransposeND mutate transpose-array X for n dims, b bits, converting between linear bh_index transpose form and per-axis coord. Encoder underlying citizenIdentity bh.

accepted.name: First-N primes sieve (Rosser bound)
accepted.note: sieveFirstNPrimes(n): scalar count n in, Eratosthenes sieve bounded by Rosser p_n<n(ln n+ln ln n); BODY returns out[] = first-n primes, a set of primes.

accepted.name: mtpHeads — K parallel zeta heads
accepted.note: mtpHeads(cp0): cp0 codepoint/glyph-index in (opts optional); spawns K zeta heads, each predicts trajectory→final cp scalar + Brown-Hilbert bh_coord. BODY returns heads set.

accepted.name: HRM slow-loop axis_bias cp-band map
accepted.note: HRM slow-loop shape classifier: maps (envelopeType name, agentProf name, currentCp scalar, history set) to a shape name + cp-band via deterministic string-pattern/history-axis matching; returns shapeRecord(shape, currentCp).

accepted.name: helm-worker http-proxy provider backend (z.ai/GLM attach point)
accepted.note: helm-worker http-proxy backend: takes worker config (set, holds httpUrl) and job prompt (name), POSTs prompt, returns response body (bytes) plus exitCode/durationMs (scalar).

accepted.name: Chunk PID composition (Brown-Hilbert PID naming)
accepted.note: chunk PID composition: builds `BH.NEW100B.PID.${c}.L${lane}.${topGpid.toString(16)}` from counter c (scalar), lane, and topGpid (scalar) into the Brown-Hilbert chunk pid.

accepted.name: sector_descriptor_prime_cylinder
accepted.note: sectorDescriptor: from sector derives cylinder = nthPrime(sector+1) (prime), engine lane=sector%3, glyph=sector%WIDTH, and a routed mode/class name; reuses classifyBhIndex and laneFor.

accepted.name: Cross-tower super-increasing band anchor (STE)
accepted.note: STE cross-tower band anchor: for each tower prime p, W=p^3, Sidon set erdosTuran(p) scaled by W, then a GUARD=2^40 super-increasing accumulator (scalar) lays anchors so addresses never cross-collide. Inputs: tower primes, Sidon set, GUARD scalar; emits the linear band addresses (bh_index) and the full collision-free address set.

accepted.name: Stacked two-layer identity scheme (AddressID / SliceDistanceID / RelationKey)
accepted.note: Stacked identity scheme: AddressID=PID=sha256(canonical(A))[0:16] (pid from bytes), SliceDistanceID=H(ProjectionCert,d) over a distance scalar and the cert hash, RelationKey/RelationEvent/ProjectionCert = H(...) geometry-free names. Given canonical bytes, a distance scalar, and the cert hash, returns the pid and the derived relation hashes.

accepted.name: timedLoop checksum (modular)
accepted.note: timedLoop: runs scalar iteration-count times, each step consuming a per-iteration byte payload (byteLength accumulated mod 1e9+7) to fold a checksum and timing scalars; returns scalar checksum/timings.

accepted.name: benchmark size/speedup ratios
accepted.note: benchmark: from a vector dimension plus raw and fast packet byte sizes, computes scalar size/speedup ratios and a sha16 fingerprint hash over the result packet.

accepted.name: fast packet zero-loss boundary
accepted.note: zero-loss boundary invariant: asserts receipt_zero_loss holds iff the payload_hash is preserved (numeric quantization approximate); reduces a hash-equality check to a scalar boolean.

accepted.name: target truth boundary
accepted.note: target truth boundary invariant: composes the ZERO_COUNTERS set with boolean truth-boundary flags (cpu active, gpu inactive) into an emitted boundary set.

accepted.name: BEHCS native-space room coordinate
accepted.note: BEHCS native-space room: maps a room name/id to its 6-axis coordinate tuple, a BEHCS glyph, and its BH.SPACE pid.

accepted.name: HRM input embedding scaling
accepted.note: HRM input embedding scaling: from hidden_size scalar computes embed_scale=sqrt(hidden_size) and init_std=1/embed_scale, then initializes scaled token/puzzle/position embedding vectors.

accepted.name: ACT halting decision + exploration
accepted.note: ACT halting: new_steps scalar vs halt_max_steps, plus q_halt/q_continue logit vectors; body returns the halted boolean mask (set of halted slots).

accepted.name: ACT bootstrap target-Q
accepted.note: Bootstrap target-Q: sigmoid of max(next_q_halt_logits, next_q_continue_logits) gated by is_last_step; two logit vectors in, target_q_continue vector out. Depends on halting step state.

accepted.name: q_halt accuracy metric
accepted.note: q_halt accuracy metric: counts where (q_halt_logits>=0)==seq_is_correct under valid mask; logit vector + correctness set in, scalar count out.

accepted.name: PID-distance cylinder-stack metric (per-prime k)
accepted.note: PID-distance cylinder-stack: pidToCylinderStack(pid,k) hashes pid per prime-index i in 1..k into {radius,angle,height} -> vector of k cylinders; distanceBetweenPIDs returns a scalar distance.

accepted.name: Brown-Hilbert dimensionality 47D live / 50D proposal
accepted.note: Brown-Hilbert dimensionality spec (live=47D, proposal=50D): folds a high-D embedding vector into a single Brown-Hilbert linear index per hyperglyph-map.public.v1.json.

accepted.name: Hookwall gate composition (reverse-gain GNN)
accepted.note: Hookwall gate composition: pipes raw message bytes through GC+super-gulp+reverse-gain-GNN+omnishannon+omnihermes+omniGNN+crop-paper, yielding a gated GNN feature vector (behcs-bus-4947).

accepted.name: 100B lazy-mint PID formula (canon)
accepted.note: 100B lazy-mint: mintPID(index)=sha16(Hilbert -> actor.device.lane.prime). Takes a linear Hilbert index plus lane and prime, hashes to a 16-hex pid. Cold tier = formula-only/regenerable.

accepted.name: Provenance self-describing tuple fields
accepted.note: provenance(): builds the self-describing tuple (set of who/what/where/when/why/how + machine) and mints its pid via mintPID(actor,{device,lane,prime}). who/device collapse to name; returns the full record (set) carrying a pid field.

accepted.name: prime-sector allocator address
accepted.note: prime-sector allocator address format BH.SECTOR.P{prime}.R{room}.{sha16}: composes a sector, a prime, and a 16-hex id (the sha16/pid slot) into the canonical Brown-Hilbert address string (name).

accepted.name: store interface contract
accepted.note: Page-store interface: put(pid,value)/get(pid)/scanByPID(prefix)/compact(never-delete). Keyed by pid with byte values; scan/compact yield collections (set). Primary signature is pid-keyed put.

accepted.name: wave-template dimensions
accepted.note: state() constant descriptor: template_count and per-template/spindle/row scalars plus chain_id_format name string; no inputs.

accepted.name: HBPv1 row authority gate vector
accepted.note: Constant HBPv1 authority row: carries a PID-tuple and a terminal row_hash with all execute/export flags=0 (describe_only); emits pid + row_hash.

accepted.name: vonMangoldtNext weighted divisor descent
accepted.note: algorithm: vonMangoldtNext(cp,opts). cp=scalar, opts=set (seed/step). Computes divisors of cp, weights each by von Mangoldt Lambda(q)/log(cp), deterministic weighted descent picks a divisor and returns {next scalar, divisor (prime-power divisor), weight scalar}.

accepted.name: whiteroomCubeAddress 3x6x6
accepted.note: whiteroomCubeAddress: one envelope (set) -> sha256 hex hash -> layer=sha%3, axis1=sha%6, axis2=sha%6 forming cube_address [layer,axis1,axis2] = a 3D coord. Body returns {sha, cube_address}; primary product is the coord.

accepted.name: quant-bus G1/G2/G3/G4 composition
accepted.note: quant-bus G1/G2/G3/G4 composition: one env (set) -> G2 mineForward path_score scalar (+ gnn_forward_quant sha16 hash), G3 mineReverseGain reverse_gain scalar (717f...) -> gnn_reverse_quant hash, G4 transition (f960...) next glsm state name. Aggregates scalar metrics + state name + sha16 quant hashes onto env.

accepted.name: hyperbehcs-gnn-drain forward+reverse
accepted.note: gnnForward(env): caller_pid(pid)+target/verb(name)+cube_47d(vector) -> node_embedding via sha16(=hash) and edge_weight = sha16(antecedents) top-4-hex/65535 (=scalar in [0,1]). reverseGainGnn extends edge_weight by priority into gain_magnitude(scalar). Body returns {node_embedding:hash, edge_weight:scalar}.

accepted.name: Brown-Hilbert prime-log embedding + tower rotation
accepted.note: Brown-Hilbert prime-log embedding + tower rotation: maps slot index n (scalar) under tower profile rho_tau (name) over the prime axes (prime, contributing log p_i/p_i) to the d-dim embedding x_i(n,tau) (vector), which projects to a Brown-Hilbert point (coord). Cross-tower squared distance D-squared is a derived scalar from the vectors.

accepted.name: D11 proof-level stamp catalog
accepted.note: D11 proof-level stamp catalog: the body is the levels object (set of PROVEN/OBSERVED/INHERITED/ASSUMED keyed by name). Stamps a claim identifier (name) against the catalog, returning the assigned proof-level (name) drawn from the level set; the catalog itself is the set.

rejected.name: Port = prefix-tree address (N^K space, O(K) walk)
BEHCS-1024 PID minter + sha16 fingerprint (Rust no_std)
